where was the Pakistan resolution session held in Lahore?

Correct Answer: Iqbal Park
Show explanation
Explanation

The resolution was presented at Minto Park, in Lahore, by Maulvi A.K. Fazlul Huq

2

How many days did the Lahore resolution session last?

Correct Answer: 3
Show explanation
Explanation

Lahore Resolution was passed in the 27th annual session of the All India Muslim League.

It was presented by A. K. Fazlul Huq.

Adopted by the All-India Muslim League on the occasion of its three-day general session in Lahore on 22–24 March 1940.

3

How many paragraphs are there in the Lahore Resolution?

Correct Answer: 5
Show explanation
Explanation

The Resolution consisted of 5 paragraphs

Resolution each paragraph was only one sentence long.

Resolution clumsily worded, it delivered a clear message.

*******

RY 14-01-2023


It was presented by A. K. Fazlul Huq, on the occasion of its three-day general session in Lahore on 22–24 March 1940.

  • **
  • Mohenjo-Daro is located in Larkana District, Sindh, Pakistan,
    • in a central position between the Indus River and the Ghaggar-Hakra River.
6

Kartatpur Border Located In Which District Of Punjab Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Narowal
Show explanation
Explanation

The Kartarpur Corridor is a visa-free border crossing and religious corridor, connecting the Gurdwara Darbar Sahib in Pakistan to Gurudwara Dera Baba Nanak

7

According to the National Accounts Committee, the growth rate of Pakistan`s economy increased during 2020-21?

Correct Answer: 5.4pc
Show explanation
Explanation
 Pakistan is the second-highest economic growth recorded in the last three years of the incumbent government. As a result of the rebasing exercise, the growth rate improved from the earlier estimate of 3.94pc to 5.4pc, while the size of the economy rose to $346.76 billion from the provisional estimate of $296 billion, according to the National Accounts Committee, which approved these figures on January 20, 2022.
8

Pakistan’s passport has been ranked as the ____ worst passport for international travel in 2022 by Henley Passport Index.

Correct Answer: Fourth
Show explanation
Explanation

The Pakistani passport has been ranked as the fourth worst passport for international travel for the third consecutive year, with visa-free or visa-on-arrival access to 31 destinations around the world, according to the Henley Passport Index 2022, released in January 2022.



9

Which credit rating agency downgraded Pakistan`s outlook to negative from stable in June 2022?

Correct Answer: Moody’s
Show explanation
Explanation

Moody`s Investor Service downgraded Pakistan`s outlook to negative from stable on June 2, 2022.

Moody`s: New York-based credit rating agency

 

10

Pakistan emerges as _______largest economy in August 2022.

Correct Answer: 24th
Show explanation
Explanation

The structure of economy has drastically changed since Pakistan`s inception in 1947 with industry and then services sectors dominated the economy, says a finance ministry report released on August 13, 2022.

 

 

11

Pakistan's national income increased by 37 % against the stipulated target of 24% in ----- five-year plan.

Correct Answer: third
Show explanation
Explanation

Pakistan's national income increased by 37 % against the stipulated target of 24% in 3rd five-year plan.

***

The national income of a country can be measured by three alternative methods:

(i) Product Method

(ii) Income Method, 

(iii) Expenditure Method.


12

the largest percentage of Pakistan is employed by which of the following sectors?

Correct Answer: Agriculture
Show explanation
Explanation
  • the largest percentage of Pakistan is employed by the Agriculture sectors
  • **
  • Agriculture constitutes the largest sector of our economy.
  • The majority of the population, directly or indirectly, is dependent on this sector.
  • It contributes about 24 percent of Gross Domestic Product (GDP)
  • and accounts for half of the employed labor force and is the largest source of foreign exchange earnings.
13

according to the particular formula, Pakistan was entitled to receive 1 billion but the Indian government agreed to pay only------at the time of independence.

Correct Answer: 0.75 billion
Show explanation
Explanation

according to the particular formula, Pakistan was entitled to receive 1 billion but the Indian government agreed to pay only  0.75 billion at the time of independence.

**

22

Who ended Khilafat Movement?

Correct Answer: Mustifa Kamal Ataturk
Show explanation
Explanation

The Khilafat movement started in 1919 by Maulana Muhammad Ali Jauhar and Maulana Shaukat Ali 

The Khilafat movement ended in 1924 by Mustafa Kamal Ataturk

The Khilafat movement started in India during World War 1 

For the purpose to protect the Khalafat

The Ottoman Caliphate was abolished on 3 March 1924  by decree of the Grand National Assembly of Turkey.

The process was one of Atatürk's Reforms following the replacement of the Ottoman Empire with the Republic of Turkey.

26

Where was Pakistan National poet Allama Iqbal born?

Correct Answer: Sialkot
Show explanation
Explanation

Know about Allama Iqbal

Born: November 9, 1877 (Sialkot)

Father: Sheikh Noor Muhammad

Mother: Imam Bibi

Received PhD: Germany

Title: Poet of the East

Famous books: Bang-e-Dara, Zarb-i-Kalim, Armughan-e-Hijaz

Famous Punjabi Books/Poem: Piyaara Jedi, Baba Bakri Wala

Died: 21 April 1938

Burial: Badshahi Mosque in Lahore

31

What was the title of Allama Iqbal’s PHD Thesis?

Correct Answer: The Development of Metaphysics in Persia
Show explanation
Explanation

Allama Iqbal's PhD thesis, submitted to the University of Munich (Germany) in 1908 and published in the same year.

It was titled "The Development of Metaphysics in Persia".

Iqbal qualified for a scholarship from Trinity College, University of Cambridge, and obtained a Bachelor of Arts in 1906.

32

List of Quaid-e-Azam fourteen points

Correct Answer: Answer check in details
Show explanation
Explanation

               List of Jinnah fourteen points (presents in 1929 in response of )

Title                               Description
Point 1:Federal System
Point 2:Provincial Autonomy
Point 3:Representation of Minorities
Point 4:Number of Muslim Representative
Point 5:Separate Electorates
Point 6:Muslim Majority Provinces
Point 7:Religious Liberty
Point 8:Three-Fourth Representation
Point 9:Separation of Sindh
Point 10:Introduction of Reforms in N.W.F.P and Baluchistan
Point 11:Government Services
Point 12:Protection of Muslim’s culture and Language
Point 13:One-Third Muslim Ministers
Point 14:Constitution

Title                                Description
Point 1: Federal System
Point 2: Provincial Autonomy
Point 3: Representation of Minorities
Point 4: Number of Muslim Representative
Point 5: Separate Electorates

38

Pakistan's deforestation rate is ____ highest in Asia.

Correct Answer: Second
Show explanation
Explanation

World Wildlife Fund (WWF) on August 14, 2020, with only 5.7pc of land, or around 4.54 million hectares under forest cover, the country’s deforestation rate is the second highest in Asia, after Afghanistan and is well below the recommended cover of 25pc.

About WWF:
The World Wide Fund (WWF) for Nature is an international non-governmental organization founded in 1961 that works in the field of wilderness preservation and the reduction of human impact on the environment.

Headquarters located in Switzerland.

45

Changa Manga Forest covers area about:

Correct Answer: 48.6 sq km (12,000 acres)
Show explanation
Explanation

the forest covers an area of 48.6 square kilometers (12,000 acres).

n Pakistan term, Changa Manga politics is used for horse trading

The Changa Manga is a planted forest and a wildlife park in Kasur District and Lahore District.

Established: 1866

the oldest hand-planted forest in the world.

The name of the forest is derived from an amalgamation of the names of two brother dacoits (bandits), Changa and Manga

The Changa Manga forest can be entered from a road off the N-5 Highway

Who has recently inaugurated the first Urban Forest project?

Show explanation
Explanation

State Bank Governor Dr Reza Baqir on October 20, 2020 inaugurated the first Polyculture Forest project, envisaging an “urban forest” on 5,000 square met­r­­es of land on the premises of Pakistan Security Print­ing Corporation, Karachi.

Pakistan has ____% area under forest?

Sher Khan was martyred on 5 July 1999 during Kargil War between India & Pakistan.

In which year was the Battle of Saragarhi fought?

The Battle of Saragarhi was fought between the British Punjabi Soldiers and  Afghan tribesmen on 12 September 1897.

Correct Answer: Aurangzeb and Dara Shikoh

Explanation

Battle of Dharmat between Aurangzeb/Mughal and Jaswant Singh, a commander of Dara Shikoh.

Battle of Dharmat Start from April 15, 1658

Location: Ujjain Madhya Pradesh


RK4-12-22


52

In which battle British established in India___?

Correct Answer: Battle of Plassey
Show explanation
Explanation
  • war of Plassey, (23 June 1757). Victory for the British East India company inside the war of Plassey become the start of nearly two centuries of British rule in India.
  • For an occasion with such momentous effects, it turned into a fairly unimpressive military come across, the defeat of the Nawab of Bengal owing an awful lot to betrayal.
  • In India, Britain turned into represented by the British East India enterprise, a challenge that have been given a royal charter in 1600 to pursue trade inside the East Indies that protected the right to shape its very own army.
  • The French East India organization had a similar remit. From 1746, the rival agencies fought the Carnatic Wars for advantage in India, wherein they maintained trading posts, and sought impact over local rulers.
53

In which of the following years, the Battle of Buxar was fought ____________?

Correct Answer: 1764
Show explanation
Explanation

The Battle of Buxar was fought on 22/23 October 1764, between the forces under the command of the British East India Company.

54

When Arabs under Muhammad bin Qasim's command reached Daibul?

Correct Answer: 712
Show explanation
Explanation

Mohammad Bin Qasim entered Daibul in 712 AD.

As a result of his efforts, he succeeded in capturing Daibul

Qasim defeated Dahir, the ruler of Sind, and killed him in the battle

**

Muḥammad ibn al-Qāsim al-Thaqafī was an Arab military commander in service of the Umayyad Caliphate

 From 708 to 711, Muhammad ibn al-Qasim led the Sindh conquest.

Muhammad ibn al-Qasim Born: 31 December 695 Taif, Hejaz,

He established Islamic rule throughout the region, serving as governor of Sindh from 712 until his death in 715.

died in 715 AD at the age of 20

Muhammad Bin Qasim built the city of Mansura as his capital

ND15-10-2023




55

The Mutiny Revolt against British Raj took place in the year?

Correct Answer: 1857
Show explanation
Explanation

Indian Mutiny, also called Sepoy Mutiny or First War of Independence

Indian Mutiny was a widespread but unsuccessful rebellion against British rule in India in 1857–59.

Location: Agra Delhi India Kanpur Lucknow

Participants: Mughal dynasty United Kingdom

Charles John Canning, Earl Canning was the governor-general of India during the Indian Mutiny of 1857.

56

17th attack of Sultan Mehmood Ghazni on India was took place in:

Correct Answer: 1027
Show explanation
Explanation

Mahmud of Ghazni’s seventeen successive raids took place from around 1000 AD until 1026 AD.

His most famous raid was in 1025 AD, where he plundered and damaged the Somnath temple in Gujarat

Additional information 

Abu al-Qasim Mahmud ibn Sabuktigin, usually known as Mahmud of Ghazni 

He was the Sultan of the Ghaznavid Empire, ruling from 998 to 1030. 

He is usually known by his title, Yamin al-Dawla 

He was born in Ghazni in Zabulistan (Afghanistan) on 2 November 971

Died: 30 April 1030 (aged 58), Afghanistan

Burial: Mosque and Tomb of Sultan Mahmud Ghaznavi, Afghanistan

57

In which state (province) famous temple of Somnath invaded by Mahmood of Ghazni is located in?

Correct Answer: Gujarat
Show explanation
Explanation

The Somnath Temple is located in Gujarat, India.

History

Mahmud of Ghazni in 1025 AD attacked and looted the Somnath Mandir l

He was the ruler of Afghanistan.

The plundered Somnath Mandir was rebuilt by the Paramara king Bhoja of Malwa and the Solanki king Bhimdev I of Anhilwara between 1026 and 1042 AD. 

Mahmud of Ghazni, a Turkish conqueror, invaded India 17 times from 1000 to 1027 AD

58

The Hindu Raja could not control the uprising of Muslims against his oppressive rule and made an appeal to Indian government. What condition was laid down by Indian government?

Correct Answer: Accession of state with India
Show explanation
Explanation

The Indian Rebellion of 1857 was a major, but ultimately unsuccessful, uprising in India in 1857–58 against the rule of the British East India Company, which functioned as a sovereign power on behalf of the British Crown.

59

After partition a large scale Muslim massacre was planned to turn the Muslim majority into minority by the Dogra ruler. In this massacre how many innocent Muslims were killed?

Correct Answer: 2,37,000
Show explanation
Explanation

The word Kashmir was derived from the ancient Sanskrit language and was referred to as káśmīra.

60

State of Kashmir was the biggest state in the sub-continent situated in the northern part of Indo Pak continent having boundaries with

Correct Answer: All of them
Show explanation
Explanation

The state of Kashmir has boundaries with:

  • to the north and east by China (Xinjiang and Tibet),
  • to the northwest by Afghanistan (Wakhan Corridor),
  • to the west by Pakistan (Khyber Pakhtunkhwa and Punjab) 
  • to the south by India (Himachal Pradesh and Punjab). 



Jammu and Kashmir is a region administered by India as a union territory.

Which has been the subject of a dispute between India and Pakistan since 1947,

and between India and China since 1962.

61

Kashmir is disputed territory between:

Correct Answer: Pakistan and India
Show explanation
Explanation

The Kashmir conflict is a territorial conflict over the Kashmir region, primarily between India and Pakistan, with China playing a third-party role.

Explanation

Capital Muzaffarabad

Correct Answer: 8 July 2016

69

Against the arbitrary and repressive rule of Dogra dynasty a movement from the platform of Kashmir Muslim conference led by Sh. Abdullah and Ch. Ghulam Abbas was launched which resulted into the first battle for freedom fought in______.

Correct Answer: 1930
Show explanation
Explanation

The Dogra dynasty Dogra Rajput Hindu from Shiwalik Himalayas created Jammu and Kashmir when all dynastic kingdoms in India were being absorbed under East India Company. 

70

Which country rejected Indian bid to hold G20 meeting in occupied Kashmir?

Correct Answer: Pakistan
Show explanation
Explanation

On 25 June 2022, Pakistan completely rejected India’s attempt to hold the G-20 summit in occupied Jammu and Kashmir.

Note: India is planning to host the 2023 meetings of the G-20 in occupied Kashmir.

 

71

In which year the Kashmiris started their freedom movement against Dogra rule before the partition of the sub-continent?

Correct Answer: 1930
Show explanation
Explanation

The Dogra dynasty was a dynasty of Hindu Rajputs who ruled Jammu & Kashmir from 1846 to 1947.

After the First Anglo-Sikh War in 1846, the British gave Kashmir and the title of 'Maharaja' to Gulab Singh

72

A person will be called the Occupied Territory on his domicile. if he has resided in Jammu and Kashmir for---------?

Correct Answer: 15 years
Show explanation
Explanation
  • A person will be called the Occupied Territory on his domicile. if he has resided in Jammu and Kashmir for 15 years
  • ***
  • A person who has resided in Jammu and Kashmir for 15 years will now be able to call the occupied territory his or her place of domicile, the Indian government stated in a gazette notification on Wednesday.
  • The government issued new domicile rules about eight months after the abrogation of Article 370 and bifurcation of the erstwhile semi-autonomous state into two union territories, both Al-Jazeera and Gulf News reported.

civilization is the stage of human social and cultural development and organization that is considered most advanced.

85

Berlin is the city of which country ?

Correct Answer: Germany
Show explanation
Explanation

86

Pakistan has borders with ____and _____?

Correct Answer: China/Iran
Show explanation
Explanation

Pakistan is bounded by Iran to the west, Afghanistan to the northwest and north, and China to the northeast.

Pakistan's total land border is 6,774KM long

Pakistan's total land border with four countries and sea.

  1. Pakistan borders India 2,912 KM long in the east,
  2. Afghanistan border is 2,430 KM long in the west (northwest)
  3. Iran border is 909 KM long also in the west
  4. China 523 KM long border is situated in the northeast.
  5. The beach of the Arabian Sea 1042 forms its southern border. 


87

Correct Answer: Nine
Show explanation
Explanation

Neelum River location: Azad Jammu & Kashmir

Neelum River known: Kishanganga river
88

Jinnah garden is located in the ?

Correct Answer: Lahore
Show explanation
Explanation

Bagh-e-Jinnah, formerly known as Lawrence Gardens , is a historical park in the city of Lahore, Pakistan.The large green space contains a botanical garden, Masjid Dar-ul-Islam, and Quaid-e-Azam Library.

89

Area wise Sindh ranks among Pakistan's four provinces:

Correct Answer: 3rd
Show explanation
Explanation

Sindh is the third largest province of Pakistan by area and second largest province by population after Punjab.

Sindh is bordered by Balochistan province to the west and Punjab province to the north.

Sindh Capital: Karachi

Sindh occupies South Eastern part of Pakistan

90

What is Current Share of Pakistan in World Population?

Correct Answer: 3.12%
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Pakistan accounts for approximately 3.12% to 3.14% of the total global population.
  • This means roughly 1 in every 32 people on the planet resides in Pakistan.


91

“Trout fish” is found in ____

Correct Answer: Northern Areas
Show explanation
Explanation
  • “Trout fish” is found in Northern Areas
  • ***
  • The trout fishery of Pakistan prevails particularly in the NWFP and Northern Areas,
  • where two species of trout, though exotic in origin, have established themselves in rivers of this province.
  • They are brown trout (Salmo trutta) and rainbow trout (Oncorhynchus mykiss).
  • Three main rivers, Neelum, Sindh, and Jhelum (Lidder), have been the principal sources of Trout,
92

Which one of the following is not a neighboring country of Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Syria
Show explanation
Explanation

Syria is located in the Middle East while Pakistan is located in South Asia.

Pakistan shares its borders with four neighboring countries – People's Republic of China, Afghanistan, India, and Iran 

Pakistan is bordered by India to the east,

Afghanistan to the northwest,

and Iran to the west 

China borders the country in the northeast.

99

The lower Indus plain starts from

Correct Answer: Mithankot
Show explanation
Explanation

The Indus River plain is a vast expanse of fertile land, covering about 200,000 square milea, with a gentle slope from the Himalayan piedmont in the north to the Arabian Sea in the south.

The lower Indus plain lies between Mithan kot  and Arabian sea

100

Which is the second highest Glacier in the world?

Correct Answer: Siachin Glacier
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The Siachen Glacier is the second-longest non-polar glacier 
  • Location: Karakoram, Ladakh (controlled by India, claimed by Pakistan)
  • Length 76 km (47 mi) 

Additional information 

  • Longest Outside Polar Regions: Fedchenko Glacier (Tajikistan).
  • 2nd Longest Outside Polar Regions: Siachen Glacier (Karakoram).
  • 3rd Longest Outside Polar Regions: Biafo Glacier (Karakoram


101

There are __________________ federating units in Pakistan.

Correct Answer: Four
Show explanation
Explanation

The Islamic Republic of Pakistan is a Federation, consisting of four Provinces

  1. Balochistan,
  2. Khyber Pakhtunkhwa,
  3. Punjab,
  4. and Sindh

**************************

The administrative units of Pakistan comprise:

  • four provinces,
  • one federal territory,
  • and two disputed territories
  • the Islamabad Capital Territory;
  • and the administrative territories of Azad Jammu and Kashmir and Gilgit–Baltistan.

        125

        When Musharraf imposed a state of emergency in Pakistan?

        Correct Answer: November 3, 2007
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Know everything about Musharraf

        Born: Delhi (August 11, 1943)

        Graduated: Pakistan Military Academy at Kakul (1964)

        Appointed chief of army staff: October 1998

        Appointer: Nawaz Sharif

        Musharraf became 10th president of Pakistan in 2001 until 2008.

        Coup: October 12, 1999 (declared a state of emergency)

        In June 2001, Musharraf became the president of Pakistan.

        In 2006, Musharraf’s autobiography titled In the Line of Fire was published.

        In March 2007, Musharraf suspended then chief justice Iftikhar Muhammad Chaudhry.

        Musharraf imposed a state of emergency on November 3, 2007.

        Resigned from his position of army chief November 2007

        Replacer: General Ashfaq Pervaiz Kayani

        Musharraf lifted the emergency on December 15, 2007.

        In 2010, Musharraf launched his political party — All Pakistan Muslim League (APML).

        He flew to Dubai on March 17, 2016

        On December 17, 2019, a special court handed Musharraf death sentence in the high treason case (only military ruler to be sentenced to death)

        Died: 5 February 2023

        Dying place: Dubai, UAE

        Disease: amyloidosis

        Aged: 79

        Buried: Karachi

        128

        In which month Kargil war fought?

        Correct Answer: May July
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Kargil War fought from the month of May to July 1999

        The Kargil War was fought between India and Pakistan on1999 in the Kargil district of Kashmir and elsewhere along the Line of Control (LoC).

        The Kargil War is also known as the Kargil Conflict but in India, this conflict is known as Operation Vijay

        Kargil's Issue was stopped by the intervention of the United States of America (USA).

        Strength: India: 30,000 = Pakistan: 5,000

        Captain Karnal Sher Khan Shaheed Nishan-e-Haider was known as the hero of the Kargil war

        General Pervez Musharraf was the Chief of the Army Staff of the Pakistani army during the Kargil War in 1999.

        Gen VP Malik was the Chief of the Army Staff of the Indian army during the Kargil war in 1999.

        Gujranwala is home to the best quality rice variety in the world,

        It is the 5th most populous city proper after Karachi, Lahore, Faisalabad, and Rawalpindi respectively.

        The old Name Of Gujranwala is Khanpur Shansi

        The New Name Of Gujranwala is "City of Wrestlers"

        Sahibzada Mohammad Khurshid First Muslim and 3rd Governor of North-West Frontier Province (KPK) from 16 July 1949 to 14 January 1950

        142

        Malik Ghulam Muhammad was the ________ Governor General of Pakistan.

        Correct Answer: Third

        Article 101 (2) of the Constitution Governor is not less than thirty-five years of age.

        Should not be a member of Parliament 

        A Governor in Pakistan is the appointed Head of State of a province.

        The Provincial Governor is appointed by the President of Pakistan, on the advice of the Prime Minister.

        The Governor's tenure lasts for five years.

        The Lawyers' Movement, also known as the Movement for the Restoration of Judiciary or the Black Coat Protests,

        The Lawyers' Movement was the popular mass protest movement initiated by the lawyers of Pakistan in response to the former president and army chief Pervez Musharraf's actions of 9 March 2007

        Date: 9 March 2007 — 17 March 2009

        Location: Nationwide, throughout Pakistan

        Caused by: Suspension of chief justice Iftikhar Muhammad Chaudhry

        Bangladesh, June 2E —Prime Minister Zulfikar Ali Bhutto of Pakistan publicly apologized,  for what he called the

        “shameful repression and unspeakable crimes”

        committed in Bangladesh by the Pakistani Army before the eastern part of Pakistan gained independence in December 1971.

        All important question "INDUS RIVER"

        The Indus River starts in Tibet

        The river Indus originates from the Mansarovar Lake

        The Indus River flows through India, Pakistan,

        The Indus River finally falls into the Arabian Sea near Karachi.

        River Indus starts making a delta near the city of Thatta

        The old name of the Indus River was: Sindhu

        National River of Pakistan: Indus River

        6 Barrages constructed in the Indus River

        159

        Tell the name of the best leader in the subcontinent who brought administrative reforms _____?

        Correct Answer: Sher Shah Suri
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Sher Shah Suri was the founder of the Suri Empire in India, with its capital in Sasaram in modern-day Bihar.

        He introduced the currency of rupee.

        An ethnic Afghan, Sher Shah took control of the Mughal Empire in 1538. After his accidental death in 1545, his son Islam Shah became his successor.


        160

        A contemporary Mughal historian, who prepared a list of charges against Akbar and called him the enemy of Islam, was:

        Correct Answer: Badayuni
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Answer:

        A

        Abdul Qadir Bada'uni 

         Abdul Qadir Bada'uni (1540 – 1615) was a famous Mughal historian who flourished in the court of Akbar. He did not accept Akbar's religious views. As an Orthodox Sunni, Badauni made a list of accusations against Akbar and called him an enemy of Islam.

        He was a poet, musician, and calligrapher, more an aesthete than a political leader.

        Bahadur Shah Zafar died November 7, 1862, in Rangoon [now Yangon], Myanmar.

        168

        Pakistan’s largest oil fields are located in District:

        Correct Answer: Attock
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • Pakistan’s largest oil fields are located in District: Attock

        • KHAUR oil field lies 54 miles southwest of Rawalpindi in Campbellpur (now Attock) District.
        • This is the oldest field in Pakistan, discovered by the Attock Oil Company in 1915.
        • **
        • JOYA MAIR oil field was discovered in 1944 by the Attock Oil Company.
        • BALKASSAR oil field is located west of Joya Mair in Jhelum District. The first well was drilled by Attock Oil Company in 1945/6.
        • The KARNAL oil field was the first field discovered after independence.
        • ***
        • Pakistan has found new deposits of oil and gas in the exploratory well Mamikhel South-01, located in Tal block in KPK
        • Byco is the single largest oil refinery in Pakistan.

        The Lakhra coalmine is spread over an area of 1,309sq km

        The Lakhra coal field is located on the west side of the Indus River Valley 

        Lakhra town is famous for its Coal

        The mine has coal reserves amounting to 1.33 billion tonnes of coking coal

        Lakhra Coal field one of the largest coal reserves in Pakistan

        198

        Shah Jahan the Mughal emperor was removed from The Throne by

        Correct Answer: Aurangzeb
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Aurangzeb, Shah Jahan's son, removed him from the throne in 1658.

        Aurangzeb was victorious in a bloody succession struggle with his brothers.

        He imprisoned Shah Jahan in Agra Fort until his death in 1666. 

        History

        In September 1657, Shah Jahan fell ill and appointed his eldest son, Dara Shikoh, as his successor. 

        This led to a succession crisis among Shah Jahan's four sons. 

        Aurangzeb, with the support of the Islamic orthodoxy, defeated Dara in 1659 and had him executed. 

        Aurangzeb imprisoned Shah Jahan in Agra Fort until he died in 166.


        199

        In which year did Prince Salim ascend the Mughal throne?

        Correct Answer: 1605
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Prince Salim ascended the Mughal throne after the death of Mughal Emperor Akbar in 1605.

        Wakhan is the area that separates Pakistan from the Central Asian States

        It is about 350 km long

        what is the eldest son of king Shah Jahan?

        • Muhammad Dara Shikoh
        • Father Shah Jahan

        • Religion Sunni Islam
        • Born 20 March 1615 Ajmer, Rajputana, Mughal Empire
        • Died            30 August 1659 (aged 44)
        • Delhi, Mughal Empire

        • Shah Jahan's real name: Khurram (who built the Taj Mahal
        • Shah Jahan was the king of 22 provinces
        • Shah Jahan became emperor of India in 1628
        • Shah Jahan died in 1666
        • Shah Jahan Mosque is located in Thatta
        204

        Which of the following Englishmen was honored by Mughal king Jahangir with the title of Khan?

        Correct Answer: William Hawkins
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Hawkins travelled to Agra to negotiate consent for a factory from Emperor Jahangir in 1609. 

        Hawkins was given title of English Khan by Jahangir. 

        205

        Nine Nobles (Ratan) of Mughal King ________ are famous.

        Correct Answer: Akbar
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Nine Nobles of Mughal king Akbar were as follows:

        1.        Raja Birbal

        2.       Miyan Tansen

        3.       Abul Fazal

        4.       Faizi

        5.       Raja Man Singh

        6.       Raja Todar Mal

        7.       Mullah Do Piazza

        8.       Fakir Aziao-Din

        9.       Abdul Rahim Khan-I-Khana


        AGM 08-01-2023


        206

        Who was the first Muslim Governor of Delhi?

        Correct Answer: Qutbuddin Aibak
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Qutub-ud-din-Aibak was the first sultan (ruler) of Delhi. 

        He ruled Delhi from 1206 to 1210 under the banner of Mamluk dynasty.

        207

        The official language of Delhi Sultanate was:

        Correct Answer: Persian
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Persian, also known by its endonym Farsi, or "Parsi".

        It is primarily spoken in Iran, Afghanistan, and Tajikistan, and some other regions which historically were Persianate societies.

        Persian was also declared as the official language of Mughals by Akbar.

        208

        Abul Fazl the celebrated author of Akbar Nama, was murdered by:

        Correct Answer: Bir Singh Deva Bundela
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Abu'l Fazl was assassinated in 1602 while he was returning from the Deccan 

        He was killed by Bir Singh Deva Bundela 

        This assassination was ordered by Prince Salim (later Emperor Jahangir)


        He served as the grand vizier of the Mughal Empire from his appointment in 1579, until he died in 1602

        He was written by an Indian writer,

        His notable works include the:

        • Akbarnama
        • Ain-i-Akbari
        • Ruqaat
        • Insha-i-Abul Fazl
        • Persian translation of the Bible


        209

        The real name of Muhammad Shah Rangeela (The last Mughal king to sit on Peacock Throne) ?

        Correct Answer: Roshan Akhtar
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Muḥammad Shah was the 13th Mughal emperor

        Muḥammad Shah reigned from 1719 to 1748

        Muhammad Shah was the last Mughal Emperor to sit on the throne

        The real name of Muhammad Shah Rangeela was Roshan Akhtar

        Born: August 7, 1702, Ghazni, Afghanistan

        Died: April 6, 1748, in Delhi, India

        Burial: Mausoleum of Muhammad Shah, Nizamuddin Dargah, Delhi
        ND23-2-2023
        210

        Fort of Noor Jehan is located at

        Correct Answer: Lahore
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The Tomb of Nur Jahan is a 17th-century mausoleum in Lahore

        Nur Jahan, born on 1577

        Died on – 18 December 1645 

        Noor Jehan was the wife and chief consort of the Mughal emperor Jahangir 

        She achieved unprecedented political power for a woman in Mughal India

        ****

        also Remember it

        Noor Jahan Singer's Resting place is Gizri Graveyard, Karachi, Sindh.

        ND10-1-2023


        211

        Who was the last Mughal Emperor sit on the peacock throne-----------.

        Correct Answer: Muhammad Shah
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Muḥammad Shah was the 13th Mughal emperor

        Muḥammad Shah reigned from 1719 to 1748

        Muhammad Shah was the last Mughal Emperor to sit on the throne

        The real name of Muhammad Shah Rangeela was Roshan Akhtar

        Born: August 7, 1702, Ghazni, Afghanistan

        Died: April 6, 1748, in Delhi, India

        Burial: Mausoleum of Muhammad Shah, Nizamuddin Dargah, Delhi
        ***
        while

        Shahjahan was the first Mughal Emperor to sit on the Peacock Throne.

        In February 1739, Nadir Shah captured Delhi and took away the Peacock Throne with Muhammad Shah as the trophy of victory.

        ND23-2-2023

        212

        The Taj Mehal, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, was build by which Mughal Emperor?

        Correct Answer: Shah Jahan
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The Taj Mahal was designated as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1983 for being "the jewel of Muslim art in India and one of the universally admired masterpieces of the world's heritage"


        Taj Mahal

        Construction started

        1632

        Construction completed

        1647

        Built by

        Mughal Emperor Shah Jehan

        Built in the memory of

        Mumtaz Mahal

        Height

        73 M

        **

        ND10-1-2023

        213

        Prince Dara Shikoh, son of Emperor Shahjahan, was a disciple of

        Correct Answer: Ghulam Farid
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Dara Shikoh also known as Dara Shukoh

        was the eldest son and heir-apparent of the Mughal emperor

        Dara Shikoh was a follower of the Armenian Sufi-perennials mystic Sarmad Kashani,

        as well as Lahore's famous Qadiri Sufi saint Mian Mir,

        whom he was introduced to by Mullah Shah Badakhshi (Mian Mir's spiritual disciple and successor).

        ND5-5-2023



        214

        Who installed the 'Chain of Justice'?

        Correct Answer: Jehangir
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Mughal Emperor Jahangir was well known for his innovative policy of the 'Chain of Justice'.

        215

        The last Mughal emperor deposed by the British All India Company was?

        Correct Answer: Bahadur Shah Zafar
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Behadur Shah Zafar was the last Mughal Emperor who was imprisoned by the English in Myanmar.

        Bahadur Shah Zafar was the last Mughal emperor of India (reigned 1837–57).

        He was a poet, musician, and calligrapher, more an aesthete than a political leader.

        Bahadur Shah Zafar was born October 24, 1775, in Delhi, India

        Bahadur Shah Zafar died November 7, 1862, in Rangoon [now Yangon], Myanmar.

        216

        How many Chinese nationals killed in suicide attack at Karachi University in April_______?

        Correct Answer: Three
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        One Pakistani & three Chinese citizens among Confucius Institute Director Huang Guiping, Ding Mupeng & Chen Sai dead.

        Incident Took place: 26 April 2022 (2:30pm)

        BLA Claimed responsibility of Karachi attack.

         

        217

        On 2 December 2022, Pakistan embassy attacked in which country?

        Correct Answer: Afghanistan
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Pakistan`s embassy in Kabul, Afghanistan came under attack on December 2, 2022, leaving one security guard critically injured.

         

         

         

        218

        Pakistan observed ________ anniversary of APS Peshawar tragedy on 16 December 2021.

        Correct Answer: 7th
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The seventh anniversary of the tragic incident of terrorist attack on Army Public School Peshawar was observed on 16 December 2021.

        Around 150 martyred (132 children)


        219

        The Sindh Hari Committee was Founded in 1930 by?

        Correct Answer: G.M Syed
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The Sindh Hari Committee (landless peasant farmers) was a leftist and Sindhi nationalist political organization.
        first President of the committee         Hyder Bux Jatoi
        Became a constituent party in         1957
        220

        Sardar Ataullah Mengal was the founder of which Political Party in Pakistan?

        Correct Answer: Baluchistan National Party (BNP)
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        In May 1972, Sardar Ataullah Mengal becomes the first Chief Minister of Balochistan.

        Sardar Ataullah Mengal was the founder of the Baluchistan National Party (BNP) Political Party in Pakistan.

        Balochistan's first chief minister Sardar Attaullah Mengal passes away at 93 on 2nd September 2021


        221

        When the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan 1973, was promulgated. who was the opposition leader in national assembly on that time?

        Correct Answer: Khan Abdul Wali Khan
        Show explanation
        Explanation

         The Leader of the Opposition قائد حزب اختلاف is the leader of the largest political party in the National Assembly that is not in government.

        This is usually the leader of the second-largest political party in the National Assembly.

        222

        Which political party was founded by Dr Abdul Qadeer?

        Correct Answer: Tehreek-e-Tahaffuz-e-Pakistan
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Tehreek-e-Tahaffuz-e-Pakistan (TTP) was a political party in Pakistan founded and led by nuclear scientist Abdul Qadeer Khan.  

        In September 2013, the party was dissolved by Abdul Qadeer Khan.

        223

        The book “Political Parties in Pakistan” (1947-1958) is written by_____________?

        Correct Answer: M. Rafique Afzal
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Pakistan’s 2018 general elections marked the second successful transfer of power from one elected civilian government to another.

        224

        Under which scheme did Muslim league surrender the demand of separate electorate for the Muslims?

        Correct Answer: Delhi Proposal 1927
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Congress propagated joint electorates as to them separate electorates would have weakened the foundations of the Indian nationalism

        225

        Which Political Party was in Power in North West Frontier Province (Now KPK) at the time of Independence?

        Correct Answer: Congress
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The Indian National Congress,is a political party in India with widespread roots.

        Founded in 1885, it was the first modern nationalist movement to emerge in the British Empire in Asia and Africa.

        226

        The Hindu Mahsahaba was established in ?

        Correct Answer: 1921
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Founded: 1915

        Founders: Vinayak Damodar Savarkar, Pandit Madan Mohan Malaviya, Lala Lajpat Rai

        227

        Bilawal Bhutto Zardari became the federal minister for the first time on:

        Correct Answer: April 27th 2022

        228

        After how many years of its establishment PTI won elections.

        Correct Answer: 22
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf  is a political party in Pakistan.

        It was founded in 1996 by the Pakistani cricketer-turned-politician Imran Khan.

        229

        Former president of Pakistan Asif ali zardari anad his sister Faryal Talpur have been charges against :

        Correct Answer: Money Laundering
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Money laundering is the process of concealing the origin of money, obtained from illicit activities such as drug trafficking, corruption, embezzlement or gambling, by converting it into a legitimate source.

        It is a crime in many jurisdictions with varying definitions.

        230

        Pervez Musharraf launched his political party APML in the year_?

        Correct Answer: 2010
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Know everything about Musharraf

        Born: Delhi (August 11, 1943)

        Graduated: Pakistan Military Academy at Kakul (1964)

        Appointed chief of army staff: October 1998

        Appointer: Nawaz Sharif

        Musharraf became 10th president of Pakistan in 2001 until 2008.

        Coup: October 12, 1999 (declared a state of emergency)

        In June 2001, Musharraf became the president of Pakistan.

        In 2006, Musharraf’s autobiography titled In the Line of Fire was published.

        In March 2007, Musharraf suspended then chief justice Iftikhar Muhammad Chaudhry.

        Musharraf imposed a state of emergency on November 3, 2007.

        Resigned from his position of army chief November 2007

        Replacer: General Ashfaq Pervaiz Kayani

        Musharraf lifted the emergency on December 15, 2007.

        In 2010, Musharraf launched his political party — All Pakistan Muslim League (APML).

        He flew to Dubai on March 17, 2016

        On December 17, 2019, a special court handed Musharraf death sentence in the high treason case (only military ruler to be sentenced to death)

        Died: 5 February 2023

        Dying place: Dubai, UAE

        Disease: amyloidosis

        Aged: 79

        Buried: Karachi

         

         

        231

        Vedat Dalokay, the architecture of Faisal Mosque of Pakistan belonged to which country_____?

        Correct Answer: Turkey
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Vedat Ali Dalokay was a renowned Turkish architect and a former mayor of Ankara.

        Show explanation

        Masjid Mahabat Khan is located in _______ ?

        Correct Answer: Peshawar
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Masjid Mahabat Khan is located in:Peshawar

        Mahabat Khan masjid is a 17th-century Mughal-era mosque

        Mosque Mahabat Khan was built in 1630 and completed in 1670.

        Mahabat Khan Mosque build by Mughal emperor Shah Jahan

        Mosque name is related to Mughal Governor Peshawar “Nawab Mahabat Khan bin Ali Mardan Khan”.

        Mahabat Khan Mosque destroyed by great fire in June 1895.

        He also sought the political union of Hindus and Muslims, which earned him the title of “the best ambassador of Hindu-Muslim unity.”

        254

        Which important building in Pakistan was attacked by terrorist on 29 June 2020

        Correct Answer: Pakistan Stock Exchange
        Show explanation

        255

        The mosque of 'Dai Anga' is located in:

        Correct Answer: Lahore

        The mosque built-in 1635 in honour of wet-nurse of the Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan.

        Correct Answer: 5 years
        Show explanation

        The strategic plan has set headline targets for Islamic banking industry to be achieved by 2025.

        State Bank of Pakistan commenced operation on July 1, 1948.

        Correct Answer: Lahore
        Show explanation

        A third centre was established in Rawalpindi-Islamabad in 1965 and the fourth in Karachi in 1966.

        264

        Where is famous building Chauburji located

        Correct Answer: Lahore
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The monument was built in 1646 C.E. during the reign of the emperor Shah Jahan.

        It previously acted as a gateway to a large garden.

        Veteran Pakistani Actor Masood Akhtar passed away on the date:

        Correct Answer: 5th March 2022

        after a protracted illness at the age of 83.

        Release date: July 7, 2007 (Pakistan)

        Director: Shoaib Mansoor

        268

        First Sindhi film produced in Pakistan was__________?

        Correct Answer: Uamr Marvi
        Show explanation

        Umar Marvi, Pakistan about a village girl Marvi Maraich.

        Who resists the overtures of a powerful King and the temptation to live in the palace as a queen, preferring to be in simple rural environment with her own village folk.

        269

        Famous Pakistani Singer Ustad Nusrat Fateh Khan belonged to which city ?

        Correct Answer: Faisalabad
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        King of Qawwali, Nusrat Fateh Ali Khan died on August 16, 1997 of a sudden cardiac arrest in London at the age of 48.

        270

        The peak period for Pakistan film Industry was :

        Correct Answer: 1960 s
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The 1960s is often cited as being the golden era of Pakistani cinema.

        Many stars were introduced during this period who would go on to become cinema legends on the silver screen.


        271

        Intersection method of detailed plotting is most suitable for______?

        Correct Answer: hilly areas
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The intersection method of detailed plotting is most suitable for hilly areas.

        تفصیلی پلاٹنگ کا چوراہا طریقہ پہاڑی علاقوں کے لیے انتہائی موزوں ہے۔



        272

        Alley cropping' means:

        Correct Answer: Growing of field crops in between two widely spaced rows of fast growing trees
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Alley cropping is the planting of trees or shrubs in two or more sets of single or multiple rows with agronomic, horticultural, or forage crops cultivated in the alleys between the rows of woody plants.

        273

        The total cultivated area in Pakistan is ________?

        Correct Answer: 25%
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        In total, the agriculture sector contributes 18.5 percent to the country's GDP.

        Out of the total area of 79.6 million hectares, 22.1 million hectares are cultivated.

        The rest of the territory is comprised of culturable waste, densely populated forests and rangelands.

        274

        The Largest Cotton Exchange Building of Pakistan is situated in _____?

        Correct Answer: Karachi
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Karachi Cotton Exchange is located in Karachi, Sindh.

        It is the largest cotton trading exchange in Pakistan.

        When the period of the Kharif crop start and when end?

        Correct Answer: June-September
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Major Kharif crops are:

        1.       Rice 

        2.       Sugarcane

        3.       Cotton

        4.       Maze

        5.       Bajra

        6.       Jawar

        When the period of the Kharif crop starts and when end?

        Strat: April-June,      

        End:  Sept-Oct

        while

        When does the period of the Rabi crop start and when end?

        These crops are planted in winter from October to December

        and harvested in summer from March to May

        Which are Pakistan's major seasons for the agricultural crop?

        Kharif & Rabi

        276

        Kharif crop of Pakistan include:

        Correct Answer: All of these
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Major Kharif crops are:

        1.       Rice 

        2.       Sugarcane

        3.       Cotton

        4.       Maze

        5.       Bajra

        6.       Jawar

        When the period of the Kharif crop starts and when end?

        Strat: April-June,      

        End:  Sept-Oct

        while

        When does the period of the Rabi crop start and when end?

        These crops are planted in winter from October to December

        and harvested in summer from March to May

        Which are Pakistan's major seasons for the agricultural crop?

        Kharif & Rabi

        ND23-2-2023

        277

        Which of the following is not a Kharif crop? Jute, Maize, Mustard, Rice, All of these

        Correct Answer: Mustard
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Major Kharif crops are:

        1.       Rice 

        2.       Sugarcane

        3.       Cotton

        4.       Maze

        5.       Bajra

        6.       Jawar

        When the period of the Kharif crop starts and when end?

        Strat: April-June,      

        End:  Sept-Oct

        while

        When does the period of the Rabi crop start and when end?

        These crops are planted in winter from October to December

        and harvested in summer from March to May

        Which are Pakistan's major seasons for the agricultural crop?

        Kharif & Rabi

        ND23-2-2023

        278

        Main objectives of Operation Zarb-e-Azb include:

        Correct Answer: All of the above
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Operation Zarb-e-Azb was a joint military offensive conducted by the Pakistan Armed Forces against various militant groups, including the Tehrik-i-Taliban Pakistan, the Islamic Movement of Uzbekistan, the East Turkestan Islamic Movement, Lashkar-e-Jhangvi, al-Qaeda, Jundallah and the Haqqani network. 

        Start date: June 15, 2014

        Location: Pakistan, North Waziristan Agency, Federally Administered Tribal Area, Karachi, Balochistan, Punjab

        279

        Operation Rad-ul-Fasad started under the Chief of the Army.

        Correct Answer: Gen. Qamar Javed Bajwa
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Army Chief General Bajwa start Operation Rad-ul-Fasad

        Operation Rad-ul-Fasad is an intensive nationwide operation against terror groups

        The Radd-ul-Fasaad took place a year and a half after the completion of Zarb-e-Azb

        Started on: 22 February 2017

        It is a continuation of the National Action Plan (NAP), which ended on February 22, 2021, for four years.

        Location: Balochistan and Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan


        280

        When operation fair play occur

        Correct Answer: 1977
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Operation Fair Play occur in 1977

        ****

        Operation Fair Play was the code name for the 5 July 1977 coup by Pakistan Chief of Army Staff General Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq.

        overthrowing the government of Prime Minister Zulfikar Ali Bhutto 


        ***

        Zia deposed Bhutto in a military coup and declared martial law on 5 July 1977



          *****

          RY 4-12-2022

          281

          Operation Zalzala was started in _________

          Correct Answer: Jan 24, 2008 – May 20, 2008
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Operation Zalzala concluded with mixed results as the army had successfully captured the area, but had failed to capture or kill Qari Hussain, the main objective of the operation

          Location: South Waziristan Agency, Spinkai

          Dates: Jan 24, 2008 – May 20, 2008

          Result: Pakistani victory: Pakistan Army captured the area successfully

          282

          The Battle of Bajaur, also known as Operation Sherdil held in _________

          Correct Answer: Aug 7, 2008 – Mar 1, 2009
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The Battle of Bajaur, also known as Operation Sherdil, was a military campaign in the Bajaur region of Pakistan.

          It was conducted on 7 August 2008 by the Frontier Corps and Infantry Brigade of the Pakistan Army.

          The operation was primarily launched to end the political movement of Tehrik-e-Taliban Pakistan. 

          Operation dates: Aug 7, 2008 – Mar 1, 2009

          Location: Bajaur Agency, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa

          283

          Battle of Swat, also known as Operation Rah e Haq was started in ________

          Correct Answer: 2007 to 2009
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Battle of Swat, also known as Operation Rah e Haq consists of the following 3 operations

          Operation Rah-i-Haq-I in Swat Valley and Shangla district (2007)

          Operation Rah-i-Haq-II in Swat valley and Shangla (2008)

          Operation Rah-i-Haq-III in Swat valley and Shangla (2009)

          284

          Operation Sirat e Mustaqeem was stated in ________

          Correct Answer: Jun 28, 2008 – Jul 9, 2008
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Operation Sirat-e-Mustaqeem was a Pakistan Army operation in Bara Tehsil of Khyber Agency, FATA.

          The Army halted the operation on July 9, 2008, after liberating Bara Tehsil from the terrorist.

          Location: Federally Administered Tribal Area, Khyber District, Bara Tehsil

          Dates: Jun 28, 2008 – Jul 9, 2008

          285

          Operation Black Thunderstorm was started in ___________

          Correct Answer: Apr 26, 2009 – Jun 14, 2009
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Operation Black Thunderstorm was a military operation conducted by the Pakistan Army,

          with the aim of retaking Buner, Lower Dir, Swat and Shangla districts from the Tehrik-i-Taliban Pakistan.

          Location: Swat, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Lower Dir, Shangla

          Dates: Apr 26, 2009 – Jun 14, 2009

          286

          Mohmand Offensive also known as Operation Brekhna was started in _______

          Correct Answer: Nov 3, 2009
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The Mohmand Offensive also known as Operation Brekhna was a Pakistani military operation against the Tehrik-i-Taliban in the Mohmand Agency area of the Federally Administered Tribal Areas that began in 2009.

          Location: Mohmand Agency, Federally Administered Tribal Area

          287

          Operation Rah e Nijat was started in ________, South Waziristan.

          Correct Answer: 19 June – 12 December 2009
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Operation Rah-e-Nijat Date: 19 June – 12 December 2009 (5 months, 3 weeks and 2 days)

          Operation Rah-e-Nijat Location: South Waziristan, Federally Administered Tribal Areas, Pakistan


          288

          Operation Radd ul Fasaad was started on _________ by Army Chief Qamar Javed Bajwa

          Correct Answer: February 22, 2017
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Operation Radd-ul-Fasaad means the Elimination of Strife جھگڑے کا خاتمہ

          Operation Radd ul Fasaad was started on February 22, 2017,

          Operation Radd ul Fasaad was started by Army Chief Qamar Javed Bajwa

          The foundational aim and objective of Operation Radd-ul-Fasaad is to ensure enduring peace in the country

          289

          Pakistan Army on launched ‘Operation Radd-ul-Fasaad’ across the country on ______________?

          Correct Answer: 22nd Feb 2017
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Operation Radd-ul-Fasaad means the Elimination of Strife جھگڑے کا خاتمہ

          Operation Radd ul Fasaad was started on February 22, 2017,

          Operation Radd ul Fasaad was started by Army Chief Qamar Javed Bajwa

          The foundational aim and objective of Operation Radd-ul-Fasaad is to ensure enduring peace in the country

          290

          Which of the following is not the source of Alternate Energy?

          Correct Answer: Thermal Energy
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Thermal energy  is produced when a rise in temperature causes atoms and molecules to move faster and collide with each other. 



          291

          Pakistan negotiated a deal for importing reprocessing plant in:

          Correct Answer: 1970
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          1970

          292

          Nuclear energy when expended converts

          Correct Answer: Nuclear into thermal
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Three mutual conversions of energy forms occur at nuclear power plants:

          nuclear energy is converted into thermal energy,

          thermal energy is converted into mechanical energy,

          and mechanical energy is converted into electric energy.


          293

          Which sector is the biggest consumer of electricity in Pakistan?

          Correct Answer: Domestic
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          It can be observed that the domestic sector is the largest consumer of electricity,

          According to the Electric Sector Of Pakistan:

          The largest and biggest source of electricity generation in Pakistan is Natural gas 

          The second largest and biggest source of electricity generation in Pakistan is Hydal

          Electricity generation

          Natural gas: 32.3% of the total.

          Hydroelectric: 24.7% of the total.

          ND23-2-2023

           


          294

          On 24 March 2020 Prime Minister Imran Khan annoced __________ Economic relief.

          Correct Answer: 1200 BL
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          On 24 March 2020, Prime Minister Imran Khan announced 1200 BL (billion rupees) in economic relief measures.




          295

          The flex plant gives us which fabric

          Correct Answer: Linen
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Linen is a textile made from the fibers of the flax plant.

          Linen is very strong, absorbent, and dries faster than cotton. 

          296

          Pakistan will export onion to which country under a protocol signed on November 23, 2021?

          Correct Answer: China
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Pakistan will export onion to China under a protocol signed on November 23, 2021.

          297

          In Pakistan, manufacturing sector contributes ___ percent to Gross Domestic Product

          Correct Answer: 12.79
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          In Pakistan, manufacturing sector contributes 12.79 percent to Gross Domestic Product (GDP) and the sector employs 16.1 percent of the country's labor force.


          298

          The Iran-Pakistan gas pipeline was officially inaugurated on____________?

          Correct Answer: March 11, 2013
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Inaugurated by the president of Pakistan Asif Ali Zardari and the President of Iran Mahmoud Ahmadinejad.


          299

          On February 4, 2022, Pakistan signed accord with which country for industrial cooperation?

          Correct Answer: China
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          Pakistan`s Board of Investment (BoI) and China`s National Development and Reform Commission (NDRC) on February 4, 2022, signed the much-needed `Framework Agreement on Industrial Cooperation` that will inter alia facilitate the relocation of industries as well as investment from China and other parts of the world to Special Economic Zones (SEZs) in Pakistan.
          300

          How many regulatory bodies placed under the administrative control of the respective ministries concerned in December 2016 ?

          Correct Answer: 5
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          According to a notification issued by the cabinet division these five regulatory bodies had been placed under the ministries concerned.


          1. National Electric Power Regulatory Authority (Nepra),

          2. the Oil and Gas Regulatory Authority,

          3. Pakistan Telecommunication Authority,

          4. Frequency Allocation Board

          5. Public Procurement Regulatory Authority

          301

          How many MoU signed to boost border trade with between Iran and Pakistan in June 2022?

          Correct Answer: 32
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Pakistan and Iran on June 1, 2022 signed a 32-point memorandum of understanding (MoU) to boost bilateral trade and provide more facilities to people involved in the border trade and other business activities.

          302

          Pakistan bought Mirage Fighter Aircraft from:

          Correct Answer: France
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Pakistan bought Mirage Fighter Aircraft from France in 1967.

          303

          Which Newspaper supported Nehru Report prepared by a committee of the All Parties Conference chaired by Motilal Nehru with his son Jawaharlal Nehru acting as secretary?

          Correct Answer: Zamidar Newspaper
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Zamindar was an Urdu newspaper.

          The founding editor of this newspaper was Maulana Zafar Ali Khan.


          304

          Pakistan and Turkiye signed a Preferential Trade Agreement (PTA) in 2022, which will come into effect from:

          Correct Answer: January 1, 2023
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • Pakistan and Turkiye on 12 August 2022 signed a Preferential Trade Agreement (PTA) aimed at boosting trade between the two countries.
          • The PTA will come into effect from January 1, 2023.
          305

          Pakistan signed Immigration Return deal with which country on 17 August 2022?

          Correct Answer: UK
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • Pakistan and the United Kingdom (UK) signed the agreement that would enable London to repatriate Pakistani nationals involved in crimes and violating the immigration laws.
          • The deal was signed on 17 August 2022.
          306

          National Climate Change Gender Action Plan launched on

          Correct Answer: 20 July 2022
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          First ever National Climate Change Gender Action Plan was launched in Islamabad on 20 July 2022.

          Correct Answer: heavy government expenses abroad

          Explanation

          Identify the main cause of the adverse balance of payment in Pakistan: heavy government expenses abroad

          ****

          An adverse balance of payment is a negative and unfavorable sign for any country

          310

          When The National Finance Commission Award or NFC was enacted?

          Correct Answer: 1951
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          311

          Identify the smallest civil award of Pakistan :

          312

          The tools and weapons of Harappan Civilization were mostly made of______________?

          Correct Answer: Copper, tin and bronze
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Copper and bronzewere used to make tools, weapons.

          Harappans also made stone seals.

          313

          The Statue of the famous dancing girl was found in the Mohenjo Daro?

          Correct Answer: Sambara
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The Dancing Girl is a bronze statuette created over 4,500 years ago

          It was found in the ancient Mohenjo-Daro site in 1926.


          314

          The Great Bath of the Indus Valley Civilization was discovered in___________?

          Correct Answer: Mohenjodaro
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Mohenjo-daro is an archaeological site in the province of Sindh, Pakistan.

          Built around 2500 BCE, it was one of the largest settlements of the ancient Indus Valley Civilisation.

          Features such as standardized bricks, street grids, and covered sewerage systems.

          Founded: 26–25th century BCE

          UNESCO World Heritage Site inscription: 1980

          315

          The Great Granary of the Indus Valley Civilization has been discovered at____________?

          Correct Answer: Mohenjodaro
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Mohenjo-daro is an archaeological site in the province of Sindh, Pakistan.

          Built around 2500 BCE, it was one of the largest settlements of the ancient Indus Valley Civilisation.

          Features such as standardized bricks, street grids, and covered sewerage systems.

          Founded: 26–25th century BCE

          UNESCO World Heritage Site inscription: 1980

          316

          Indus Valley Civilization was spread over____________?

          Correct Answer: Sindh, Punjab, Jammu and Kashmir, U.P, Rajasthan, Gujarat
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The Indus Valley Civilisation, also known as the Indus Civilisation.

          Bronze Age civilisation in the northwestern regions of South Asia, lasting from 3300 BCE to 1300 BCE, and in its mature form from 2600 BCE to 1900 BCE.

          Period: Bronze Age South Asia

          Major sites: Mohenjodaro and Harpa pak

          317

          Rice cultivation is associated with Harappan site of_____________?

          Correct Answer: Lothal
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The Indus Valley Civilisation, also known as the Indus Civilisation.

          Bronze Age civilisation in the northwestern regions of South Asia, lasting from 3300 BCE to 1300 BCE, and in its mature form from 2600 BCE to 1900 BCE.

          Period: Bronze Age South Asia

          Major sites: Mohenjodaro and Harpa 

          318

          The Indus valley people knew the use of______________?

          Correct Answer: Gold, Silver, Copper, Bronze but not iron
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The Indus Valley Civilisation, also known as the Indus Civilisation.

          Bronze Age civilisation in the northwestern regions of South Asia, lasting from 3300 BCE to 1300 BCE, and in its mature form from 2600 BCE to 1900 BCE.

          Period: Bronze Age South Asia

          Major sites: Mohenjodaro and Harpa 

          319

          In Indus Valley “Alluvial Soil” was used for making?

          Correct Answer: Bricks
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Alluvial soils are soils deposited by surface water.

          320

          The Script Of Indus Valley Civilization Was_________?

          Correct Answer: Dravidian
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • The Script Of the Indus Valley Civilization Was Dravidian
          • The Indus script is a corpus of symbols produced by the Indus Valley Civilization.
          • Indus Valley Civilization was spread over Sindh, Punjab, Jammu and Kashmir, U.P, Rajasthan, Gujarat
          • The Indus Valley Civilisation (IVC), also known as the Indus Civilisation was a Bronze Age civilization 
          • lasting from 3300 BCE to 1300 BCE, and in its mature form from 2600 BCE to 1900 BCE. 
          • Major sites: Harappa, Mohenjo-Daro, Dholavira, and Rakhigarhi
          • Sh2-9-2023
          321

          The Harappan Civilization is also called as _____

          Correct Answer: Indus Valley Civilization
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The Harappan Civilization is also called as Indus Valley Civilization

          It was known as the Indus Valley Civilization as the cities flourished near the Indus River.

          322

          Harrappa near Sahiwal was center of______?

          Correct Answer: Indus valley civilization
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Harappa is an archaeological site in Punjab, Pakistan, about 24 km west of Sahiwal.

          323

          Between which of the following was the Ancient town of Taxila located?

          Correct Answer: Indus & Jhelum
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Taxila is located in between River Indus and river Jhelum (Rawalpindi, Punjab)

           MUG 10-12-2022


          324

          Which city was considered as Pearl of Gandhara Civilization??

          Correct Answer: Taxila
          Show explanation
          Explanation


            Gandhara Civilization flourished in Pakistan during the rule of Buddhist Kings.

            Taxila was considered as Pearl of Gandhara Civilization. 

            language of the Gandhara civilization was:  Prakrit

            ***

            Gandhara's language was a Prakrit or "Middle Indo-Aryan" dialect, usually called Gāndhārī.

            Hindko from Peshawar, which was the capital of Gandhara, came from Shuraseni Prakrit a language spoken in Gandhara.


              325

              The Indus Valley Houses were built of ________?

              Correct Answer: Bricks
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • The Indus Valley Houses were built of burnt Bricks. 
              • The Script Of the Indus Valley Civilization Was Dravidian
              • The Indus script is a corpus of symbols produced by the Indus Valley Civilization.
              • Indus Valley Civilization was spread over Sindh, Punjab, Jammu and Kashmir, U.P, Rajasthan, Gujarat
              • The Indus Valley Civilisation (IVC), also known as the Indus Civilisation was a Bronze Age civilization 
              • lasting from 3300 BCE to 1300 BCE, and in its mature form from 2600 BCE to 1900 BCE. 
              • Major sites: Harappa, Mohenjo-Daro, Dholavira, and Rakhigarhi

              • AGM 24-12-2022


              326

              Indus valley civilization was destroyed by _________?

              Correct Answer: Aryans
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The main occupation of the people of the Indus valley civilization was Agriculture.

              Indus valley civilization was destroyed by Aryans

              The Indus valley people knew the use of Gold, Silver, Copper, and Bronze but not iron

              The Indus Valley Civilisation, also known as the Indus Civilisation.

              The Indus Valley Civilisation (IVC)  was a Bronze Age civilization 

              Major sites: Harappa, Mohenjo-Daro, Dholavira, and Rakhigarhi

              327

              Which of the following airline’s license was canceled due to an air crash in 1949?

              Correct Answer: Pakistan airways
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Pakistan Airways also known as "Pak Air" or "Pak Airways", was an airline from Pakistan with its headquarters in Karachi. The company was founded in 1948 

              328

              Which of the following air company was the first to operate in Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: Orient airways
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              It was then that the Government of Pakistan decided to form a state-owned airline and invited Orient Airways to merge with it. The outcome of the merger was the birth of a new airline, named Pakistan International Airlines (PIA) on 11 March, 1955.


              329

              The highest official rank of the Air Force is ___________?

              Correct Answer: Air Chief Marshal
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Air marshal is a commissioned three-star military rank in Pakistan Air Force

              Air marshal is NATO's equivalent rank code of OF-8.

              Air marshal is a rank above two-star air vice marshal

              Air marshal is a below four-star air chief marshal,

              it is the second-highest rank of a military branch in uniformed services distinct from the navy and army,

              it is equivalent to the Vice-admiral of the navy and lieutenant general of the army

              *******

              ND3-12-2022

              330

              Where is Pakistan’s first ‘Greenfield International Airport’ located ?

              Correct Answer: Islamabad
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              A greenfield airport is an aviation facility with greenfield project characteristics.

              331

              Which of following country has recently been invited by Pakistan to become the third partner in CPEC.

              Correct Answer: Saudi Arabia
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Both sides reviewed the continued implementation of CPEC and its expansion to jointly agreed on priority areas.

              It is notable that in 2018, Pakistan invited Saudi Arabia to be the third ‘strategic’ partner in the CPEC.

              Now, this time, both China and Pakistan are seeking the direct involvement of a third country in this mega project.


              332

              Quaid e Azam asked three law lords from British Government in which commission

              Correct Answer: Boundary commission
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The commission—appointed by Lord Mountbatten, the final viceroy of British India

              Consisted of four members from the Indian National Congress and four from the Muslim League and was chaired by Sir Cyril Radcliffe.

              333

              Which Army Officer was the First Recipient of Nishan i Haider ?

              Correct Answer: Major Muhammad Akram Shaheed
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Captain Raja Muhammad Sarwar, the country's first recipient of Nishan-i-Haider

              Born in Singhori village in Gujjar Khan on November 10, 1910

              Sarwar joined the army in 1929 as a sepoy.

              He was commissioned in the Punjab Regiment in 1944 and served in World War II.

              In 1947, he volunteered to participate in the battle to retake Kashmir.

              334

              Who was the first military general of Pakistan

              Correct Answer: Gen. Frank Meservy
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              General Sir Frank Messervy was appointed as the first Army Commander-in-Chief of the new Pakistan Army.

              General Messervy was succeeded in this post in February 1948, by General Sir Douglas Gracey, who served until January 1951.

              336

              Pasni port is located in ____?

              Correct Answer: Balochistan
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Pakistan has three big sea ports named as Karachi Port, Muhammad Bin Qasim port, and Gwadar port.

              Port of Muhammad Bin Qasim is the oldest port while the Karachi port is the busiest one

              ++++++++++++

              The Port of Pasni is located in Pasni City in the Balochistan province of Pakistan.

              The facilities include modern fish harbour, port, and a naval base for the Pakistan Navy.

              337

              The Arabian Sea forms a Coastal area of about 700 Km with Pakistan.Three seaports Karachi port ,port Qasim and _____________port have been built on this coast

              Correct Answer: Gwadar
              Show explanation

              Pakistan has three big sea ports named as Karachi Port, Muhammad Bin Qasim port, and Gwadar port.

              Port of Muhammad Bin Qasim is the oldest port while the Karachi port is the busiest one

              Most of the southern areas of Pakistan are cold and ___________.

              Hot means گرم

              In Balochistan, deserts also exist in ____, which supports wind erosion excluding rocky mountains:
