              340

              He desert covering border areas of Mirpur and Sanghar districts is called :

              Correct Answer: Tharparkar desert
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Tharparkar also known as Thar, is a district in Sindh province in Pakistan headquartered at Mithi.

              The district is the largest in Sindh, and has the largest Hindu population in Pakistan.

              It has the lowest Human Development Index rating of all the districts in Sindh.

              341

              Which of these desert is in Skardu?

              Correct Answer: katpana
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Katpana Desert, also known as the Katpana Cold Desert

              It is a desert located near Skardu

              Situated at an elevation of 2,226 metres (7,303 ft) above sea level,


              1 Thar desert               (in Sindh)
              2 Kharan desert (in Balochistan)
              3 Katpana desert in Skardu               (Gilgit Baltistan)
              4 Thal desert in Bhakkar               (Punjab)
              5 Cholistan in Bahawalpur               (Punjab)

              342

              Which area of Pakistan is known as “Deserts Free Area”?

              Correct Answer: Northern Areas
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Northern Areas are the most spectacular and fascinating region of Pakistan.

              The three world's famous mountain ranges meet here — the Himalayas, the Karakorams and the Hindukush.

              343

              What is the name of desert which is host to the famous jeep rally in Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: Cholistan
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Cholistan Desert Jeep Rally is a rally raid type of off-road race, organised by the TDCP in Pakistan.

              The event is annually run in the Cholistan Desert venue.

              344

              Area of Thar Desert in square miles is ___?

              Correct Answer: 77,000 Miles
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              About Thar Desert

              15% located in Pakistan

              85% located in India

              Also called Friendly Desert

              Located near Badin, Sindh 

              Famous for: Sand 

              Coal also found in Thar 

              also known as Great Indian Desert

              7th largest desert on the planet

              3rd largest in Asia.

              Area: 77,000 Miles

              ****************



              FIA CBT VU PAPER 22-12-2021

               

              345

              Thal desert comprises of part of Mianwali, Sargodha, Muzaffargarh and ______:

              Correct Answer: Dera Gazi Khan
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Thal desert is situated in Punjab, Pakistan. That extends between in Indus and Jhelum River near the Pothohar Plateau.

              With a total length from north to south 190 miles,

              The desert covers the districts of Bhakkar, Khushab, Mianwali, Layyah, Muzaffargarh as well as Jhang, from the left bank of the river Jhelum.

              346

              What is the total length of Thal desert

              Correct Answer: approximately 190 miles
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              About Thal Desert

              Location: East Punjab 

              Between: Indus & Jhelum rivers 

              Length: 190 miles 

              ****************

              VU FIA CBT  14-12-2021

              347

              Pakistan’s National Dress for Woman is ?

              Correct Answer: Shirts, Shalwar and Dupatta
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The shalwar kameez is national dress of Pakistan and is worn by men and women in all Five provinces Punjab, Sindh, Balochistan, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa And Gilgit-Baltistan in the country and in Azad Kashmir.

              348

              National Reptile of Pakistan is_________?

              Correct Answer: Mugger Crocodile
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The mugger crocodile is a medium-sized broad-snouted crocodile, also known as mugger and marsh crocodile.

              Order: Crocodilia

              Phylum: Chordata

              Kingdom: Animalia

              349

              National insignia of Pakistan is__________?

              Correct Answer: Crescent and star
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              An ensign is the national flag flown on a vessel to indicate nationality.

              In its widest sense, an ensign is just a flag or other standard.


              350

              What is the National Instrument of Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: Dafli
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              What is the National Instrument of Pakistan?

              Trumpet, Trombone, Guiter, Dafli, None of these

              Dafli is a frame drum musical instrument, used in popular and classical music in Pakistan. 

              351

              Which fruit is kahnpur famous for cultivation?

              Correct Answer: Oranges
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The famous Fruit of Larkana is Guava

              352

              National Predator of Pakistan is__________?

              Correct Answer: Snow Leopard
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The snow leopard  also known as the ounce, is a felid in the genus Panthera native to the mountain ranges of Central and South Asia


              353

              Pakistan Monument Islamabad was designed by ___

              Correct Answer: Arif Masood
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Pakistan Monument is a national monument located on the western Shakarparian Hills in Islamabad, Pakistan. 

              Construction started 25 May 2004

              Completed 23 March 2007


              354

              Jasmine was chosen as national flower in which year?

              Correct Answer: 1961
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Jasmine was chosen as the national flower in 1961

              ********

              Common jasmine National flower

              Himalayan cedar National tree

              Mango (summer) and Guava (winter) National fruits

              Markhor National animal

              355

              Who was the first democratic President to complete his 5 years tenure?

              Correct Answer: Asif Ali Zardari
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Asif Ali Zardari is the president of Pakistan Peoples Party Parliamentarians and was the co-chairperson of Pakistan People's Party.

              He served as the 11th president of Pakistan from 2008 to 2013.

              356

              Muhammad Rafiq Tarar was ________ president before Pervaiz Musharaf ?

              Correct Answer: 9th
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Muhammad Rafiq Tarar is a Pakistani politician who served as the ninth president of Pakistan.

              He remain president from January 1998 until his resignation in June 2001.

              Prior to that as a senator from Punjab in 1997.

              357

              Which Person remained Chairman Wapda, chairman Sindh, Governor, SBP, and President of Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: Ghulam Ishaq Khan
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Ghulam Ishaq Khan, was a Pakistani bureaucrat who served as the seventh president of Pakistan.

              He was the founder of his namesake Ghulam Ishaq Khan Institute. 

              Born: January 20, 1915, Bannu

              Died: October 27, 2006, Peshawar

              Education: University of Peshawar

              Presidential term: August 17, 1988 – July 18, 1993

              358

              The President of Pakistan elected for a term of years?

              Correct Answer: 5
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              President of Pakistan elected for 5 years maximum for two consecutive terms. 

              Ayub Khan is the longest-serving president of Pakistan.

              During his two terms, remained in the office for the longest period with ten years and five months approximately

              ******

              The Prime Minister us elected for a term of 5 years

              363

              Under which Article President of Pakistan shall dissolve the National Assembly if so advised by the Prime Minister?

              Correct Answer: Under the Article 58 (1)
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Under Article 58 (1) President of Pakistan shall dissolve the National Assembly if so advised by the Prime Minister.

              and the National Assembly shall, unless sooner dissolved, stand dissolved at the expiration of forty-eight hours after the Prime Minister has so advised.

              364

              The president of Pakistan announced a new medal on 23rd March 2018 named as--------------?

              Correct Answer: tamgh-e-Amtiaz
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • The president of Pakistan announced a new medal on 23rd March 2018 named as tamgh-e-Imtiaz
              • ***
              • Awarded for The highest degree of service to the state, and for services to international diplomacy.
              • Status         Currently constituted
              • Sovereign President of Pakistan
              • Sovereign Prime minister of Pakistan


              365

              When Zia Ul Haq dissolved national assembly and removed the then prime minister of Pakistan____?

              Correct Answer: 1988
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Zia Ul Haq dissolved the National Assembly and removed Prime Minister Muhammad Khan Junejo under article 58(2)b of the amended Constitution on 29 May 1988.

              366

              Who was president prior to Fazal Ilahi chudhary?

              Correct Answer: Bhutto
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              5th president 1973-1978

              Succeeded_ Zia ul haq

              367

              Asif Ali Zardari took oath as 11th president on :

              Correct Answer: 9 September 2008
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Asif Ali Zardari was the co-chairperson of Pakistan People's Party.

              He served as the 11th President of Pakistan from 9 September 2008 to 9 Septmber 2013.


              368

              Pervez Musharraf served as _______ president of Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: 10th
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Know everything about Musharraf

              Born: Delhi (August 11, 1943)

              Graduated: Pakistan Military Academy at Kakul (1964)

              Appointed chief of army staff: October 1998

              Appointer: Nawaz Sharif

              Musharraf became 10th president of Pakistan in 2001 until 2008.

              Coup: October 12, 1999 (declared a state of emergency)

              In June 2001, Musharraf became the president of Pakistan.

              In 2006, Musharraf’s autobiography titled In the Line of Fire was published.

              In March 2007, Musharraf suspended then chief justice Iftikhar Muhammad Chaudhry.

              Musharraf imposed a state of emergency on November 3, 2007.

              Resigned from his position of army chief November 2007

              Replacer: General Ashfaq Pervaiz Kayani

              Musharraf lifted the emergency on December 15, 2007.

              In 2010, Musharraf launched his political party — All Pakistan Muslim League (APML).

              He flew to Dubai on March 17, 2016

              On December 17, 2019, a special court handed Musharraf death sentence in the high treason case (only military ruler to be sentenced to death)

              Died: 5 February 2023

              Dying place: Dubai, UAE

              Disease: amyloidosis

              Aged: 79

              Buried: Karachi

               

               

              369

              When General Ayub Khan resigned from his Presidentship?

              Correct Answer: 25th March 1969
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Muhammad Ayub Khan was the second President of Pakistan from 27 October 1958 – 25 March 1969

              Ayub Khan enforced a new constitution in Pakistan on 8th June 1962

              Ayub Khan was born on 14 May 1907 in Rehana, a village in Abbottabad District 

              Ayub Khan died in Islamabad in April 1974.

              Ayub Khan's election symbol was Red Rose

              He went on to study at Aligarh Muslim University (AMU)

              Ayub Khan was an army general who seized the presidency from Iskander Mirza in a coup in 1958

              ****

              nd9-2-2023


              370

              A referendum on the Islamisation policy of President Muhammad Zia Ul Haq was held in Pakistan on?

              Correct Answer: 19 December 1984.
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              A referendum on the Islamisation policy of President Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq was held in Pakistan on 19 December 1984.

              Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq took oath as President of Pakistan on 23 March 1985.

              Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq becomes 6th President of Pakistan.

              He declaration of martial law in 1977.

              He remains the country's longest-serving de facto head of state and Chief of Army Staff

              Ghulam Ishaq Khan succeeded Zia Ul Haque as President of Pakistan.

              Died on 17-08-1988 by Plane crashed 

              ****

              Yousaf Raza Gillani took the oath of office from President Pervez Musharraf on?

              Correct Answer: 24 March 2008
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              On 24 March 2008, Gillani was elected Prime Minister by Parliament, defeating his rival, Chaudhry Pervaiz Elahi of the PML-Q, by a score of 264 to 42

              Syed Yousaf Raza Gillani has become the leader of the opposition in Senate on 26-03-2021 with the support of 30 members.

              PPP = 21

              ANP= 02

              JI = 01

              FATA = 02

              Independent = 04

              Total = 30

              373

              The Supreme Court of Pakistan disqualified Prime Minister Nawaz Sharif under the Constitution's Article

              Correct Answer: Article 62(1)(f)
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Supreme Court of Pakistan disqualified Prime Minister Nawaz Sharif under the Constitution's Article  62(1)(f) on July 28, 2017.

              374

              Major-General Iskander Mirza was the great-grandson of __________?

              Correct Answer: Mir Jafar
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Maj Gen Sikandar Mirza (PM Pakistan) was great-grandson of Mir Jafar

              Syed Mir Jaffar Ali Khan Bahadur (Mir Jafar, Mir Sadiq) was a military general of Nawab Siraj ud Daula.

              he violated his oath and collaborated with British Army in the battle of Plassey in 1757 

              375

              How many members are required to make successful a no confidence motion against prime minister?

              Correct Answer: 172
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              For a no-confidence motion to be successful, the support of a simple majority — 172 of the total 342 members — in the lower house of parliament is required.

              376

              Name the first prime minister in the history of Pakistan who survived a no confidence motion?

              Correct Answer: Benazir Bhutto
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The then Prime Minister Benazir Bhutto survived the November 1, 1989 vote of no-confidence moved by Nawaz Sharif and his Islami Jamhoori Ittehad (IJI). 

              377

              Name the second prime minister in the history of Pakistan against whom a no-confidence was moved?

              Correct Answer: Shaukat Aziz
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Shaukat Aziz served as 17th prime minister of Pakistan from 20 August 2004 to 15 November 2007

              In August 2006, he survived a no-confidence move against him.

              378

              Imran Khan is the ________ Prime minister in the country’s history to seek a voluntary vote of confidence from the National Assembly:

              Correct Answer: second
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Nawaz Sharif was the first prime minister who sought a voluntary vote of confidence from the Parliament after his reinstatement was granted by the Supreme Court in 1993.

              379

              How many prime ministers of Pakistan have completed a full five-year tenure after winning the general elections?

              Correct Answer: None
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              At approximately five years and four months in total, Nawaz Sharif is the longest-serving prime minister with 3 time PM.

              Correct Answer: 8th March 2022
              Show explanation

              Yousuf Raza Gilani belongs to Multan.

              385

              Who became tenth prime minister in the history of Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: Muhammad Khan Junejo

              386

              When was Benazir First Government was elected

              Correct Answer: 2 Dec 1988
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              She was sworn in as the Prime Minister of Pakistan on 2 December 1988.

              Bhutto became the first female Prime Minister in a Muslim-majority country, as well as Pakistan's second nationally elected Prime Minister.

              Benazir Bhutto ; (21 June 1953 – 27 December 2007)

              Benazir Bhutto was a Pakistani politician who served as the 11th and 13th prime minister of Pakistan from 1988 to 1990 and from 1993 to 1996.

              Correct Answer: 5 June 2013
              Show explanation

              389

              Mohammad Ali Bogra was third ______of Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: Prime Minister
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Sahibzada Mohammad Ali Bogra was the 3rd Prime Minister of Pakistan from 17 April 1953 till 12 August 1955.

              He was the 3rd & 7th Minister of Foreign Affairs from 13 June 1962 till 23 January 1963

              Bogra was Minister of Defence from 17 April 1953  till 24 October 1954.

              390

              To which province did Hussain Shaheed Suhrwady belong to?

              Correct Answer: East Pakistan
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Hussain Shaheed Suhrawardy was a Pakistani left-wing statesman of Bengal.

              Among the  Founding Fathers of modern-day Pakistan.

              He was elected as the fifth Prime minister of Pakistan

              Served  as Prime minister from 1956 to 1957.


              391

              Before becoming pm Nawaz sharif was CM of-----------?

              Correct Answer: Punjab
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • Before becoming pm Nawaz sharif was CM of Punjab
              • ****
              • In 1981, Nawaz was appointed by President Zia as the minister of finance for the province of Punjab.
              • Backed by a loose coalition of conservatives, Nawaz was elected as the Chief Minister of Punjab in 1985 and re-elected after the end of martial law in 1988.
              • ***
              • Nawaz Sharif became the 12th prime minister of Pakistan on 1 November 1990, succeeding Benazir Bhutto.
              • He also became head of IJI.
              • The Second Nawaz Sharif ministry began on 3 February 1997,
              392

              General Qamar Javed Bajwa appointed COAS by which prime minister?

              Correct Answer: Nawaz Sharif
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Know about General Qamar Javed Bajwa

              Commissioned: 1980 (16th Battalion)

              Becomes 10th COAS: 29 November 2016

              Got extension of 3 years: 2019

              Appointed by: Nawaz Sharif

              Unit: 16th Baloch regiment

              Predecessor: General Raheel Sharif

              Retiring: 29 November 2022

               

              393

              Who was the Caretaker Prime Minister of Pakistan from November 1996 to February 1997?

              Correct Answer: Malik Mairaj Khalid
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              List of Caretaker Prime Ministers of Pakistan

               

              Number   

              Name

              Duration

              1

              Ghulam Mustafa Jatoi

              6 Aug 1990 to 6 Nov 1990

              2

              Balakh Sher Mazari

              18 Apr 1993 to 26 May 1993

              3

              Moeenuddin Qureshi

              18 July to 19 October 1993

              4

              Malik Meraj Khalid

              6 Nov 1996 to 17 Feb 1997

              5

              Muhammad Mian Soomro

              16 Nov 2007 to 24 Mar 2008

              6

              Mir Hazar Khan Khoso

              25 March 2013 to 5 Jun 2013

              7

              Nasirul Mulk

              1 June 2018 to 18 August 2018

              8

              Anwaarul Haq Kakar

              12 August 2023 till today

              394

              Prime Minister of Pakistan is entitled to hold his/her office for a term of_______ Years?

              Correct Answer: 5
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The prime minister of Pakistan is the head of government of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan.

              Prime Minister was to hold office for 5 years.

              The prime minister is often the leader of the party  with a majority in:

              the lower house of the Parliament of Pakistan the National Assembly 

              ****

              ND8-1-2023


              395

              When Nawaz Sharif took oath for the second time as a prime minister?

              Correct Answer: 1997
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Mian Muhammad Nawaz Sharif  served as the prime minister of Pakistan for three non-consecutive terms.

              first duration of Nawaz Sharif 1990-1993

              second duration 1997-1999

              third duration 2013-2017


              AGM 08-01-2023



              396

              Who was the founder of Pakistan Peoples Party _______?

              Correct Answer: Zulifqar Ali Bhutto
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Zulfikar Ali Bhutto served as the 9th Prime Minister of Pakistan from 1973 to 1977.

              Fourth President of Pakistan from 1971 to 1973.

              He was also the founder of the Pakistan People's Party and served as its chairman until his execution in 1979. 

              Pakistan people party was established by zulfiqar Ali butoo in 1967.


              RY29012023


              397

              Pervez Musharraf was appointed Army Chief by which prime minister_?

              Correct Answer: Nawaz Sharif
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Know everything about Musharraf

              Born: Delhi (August 11, 1943)

              Graduated: Pakistan Military Academy at Kakul (1964)

              Appointed chief of army staff: October 1998

              Appointer: Nawaz Sharif

              Musharraf became 10th president of Pakistan in 2001 until 2008.

              Coup: October 12, 1999 (declared a state of emergency)

              In June 2001, Musharraf became the president of Pakistan.

              In 2006, Musharraf’s autobiography titled In the Line of Fire was published.

              In March 2007, Musharraf suspended then chief justice Iftikhar Muhammad Chaudhry.

              Musharraf imposed a state of emergency on November 3, 2007.

              Resigned from his position of army chief November 2007

              Replacer: General Ashfaq Pervaiz Kayani

              Musharraf lifted the emergency on December 15, 2007.

              In 2010, Musharraf launched his political party — All Pakistan Muslim League (APML).

              He flew to Dubai on March 17, 2016

              On December 17, 2019, a special court handed Musharraf death sentence in the high treason case (only military ruler to be sentenced to death)

              Died: 5 February 2023

              Dying place: Dubai, UAE

              Disease: amyloidosis

              Aged: 79

              Buried: Karachi

               

               

              398

              The first prime minister of Pakistan from Bangladesh

              Correct Answer: Khwaja Nazimuddin
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Born: July 19, 1894, Dhaka, Bangladesh

              Died: October 22, 1964, Dhaka, Bangladesh

              Previous offices: Prime Minister of Pakistan 1951–1953

              Party: All-India Muslim League

              *******


              399

              Which family build Altit fort?

              Correct Answer: Mirs
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Altit Fort is a fort at Altit town in the Hunza valley in Gilgit Baltistan.

              400

              What is the name of the famous fort located in Hunza other than Altit fort?

              Correct Answer: Baltit
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Baltit Fort is a fort in the Hunza valley, near the town of Karimabad, in the Gilgit-Baltistan region of northern Pakistan. Founded in the 8th CE, it has been on the UNESCO World Heritage Tentative list since 2004.

              401

              The faiz mehal is located in__________?

              Correct Answer: khairpur
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Faiz Mahal is a palace in Khairpur, Sindh, Pakistan.

              It was built by Mir Sohrab Khan in 1798 as the principal building serving as the sovereign's court for the royal palace complex of Talpur monarchs of the Khairpur dynasty.

              402

              The small Fort within Rani Kot Fort is known as:

              Correct Answer: Mari Kot
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Miri Fort is about 3 km from the Sann gate, and is reported to have served as the palace of the Mir royal family.

              The entire fort structure has been built with stone and lime mortar.

              Location: Jamshoro District, Sindh, Pakistan

              Length: 31 km 



              403

              Fort Munro was built as a summer resort by British officials in_____________?

              Correct Answer: 1920
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Fort Munro, formerly known as Loilaresir, is a hill station which is located at a height of 6,470 feet.

              Located in Dera Ghazi Khan District.


              404

              In which city of Pakistan is Nooe Mehal located?

              Correct Answer: Bahawalpur
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Noor Mahal  is a Pakistan Army-owned palace in Bahawalpur, Punjab, Pakistan.

              It was built in 1872 like an Italian chateau on neoclassical lines, at a time when modernism had set in.

              405

              In which city PM Imran Khan to launch the tourist project 'Al-Beruni Radius'?

              Correct Answer: Jhelum
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Prime Minister Imran Khan has launched Al-Beruni Radius, a tourism project in Jhelum.

              The historical Nandana Fort is located in Tehsil Pind Dadan Khan of district Jhelum.

              According to PM's office, the prime minister is keen on the preservation and conservation of the site, aimed at converting it into an international tourism site.

              The site's archeological significance dates back to the 11th century when famous scholar Abu Rehan Al-Beruni during his stay quantified the circumference of the Earth.

              Al-Beruni later wrote a famous book about the region and mentioned Nandana as a great center of learning.

              406

              Lahore Fort and Attock fort were built by:

              Correct Answer: Akbar
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Attock fort was built by the Mughal Emperor Akbar in 1581 to support his own wars in Afghanistan.

              Mughal Emperor Akbar built the Lahore Fort, as well as the city walls which had 12 gates.

              Campbellpur is the old name of Attock. 


              407

              Hindu's' resentment towards the Urdu language and Muslim education led

              Correct Answer: Sir Syed Ahmed Khan to establish educational and research institutions for Muslims.
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Sir Syed Ahmed Khan’s most notable contribution to the field of education is establishing the Madarsatul Uloom in Aligarh in 1875.

              408

              The Saraiki language is more dominantly spoken in ________ ?

              Correct Answer: South Punjab
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              409

              Sir Syed Ahmad Khan set up a scientific society, which translated the modern work from English to which language?

              Correct Answer: Urdu
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              On January 9, 1864 Sir Syed formed a translation society called Scientific Society at Ghazipur with the goal of translating scientific books of English and other European languages into Urdu and Hindi.

              410

              Sir Syed Ahmad Khan set himself to the task of protecting which language?

              Correct Answer: Urdu
              Show explanation
              Explanation

               Sir Syed to further advocate Urdu as the symbol of Muslim heritage and as the language of all Indian Muslims.

              411

              How many percentage of Pakistani can speak or understand urdu?

              Correct Answer: More than 95%
              Show explanation
              Explanation

               The figure for Pakistan includes only native Urdu-speakers, known as Muhajirs, and not other ethnic groups of Pakistan who may fluently speak Urdu as a first or second language.

              412

              Pushto letters of alphabet were prepared by _________?

              Correct Answer: Saifullah
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Pashto has ی, ې, ۀ, and ۍ for additional vowels and diphthongs as well.

              Pashto uses all 28 letters of the Arabic alphabet, and shares 3 letters (چ, پ, and ژ) with Persian and Urdu in the additional letters.

              413

              Sindhi letters of alphabet were prepared by __________?

              Correct Answer: Aben-al-Hassan
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              It has a total of 52 letters, augmenting the Persian with digraphs and eighteen new letters (ڄ ٺ ٽ ٿ ڀ ٻ ڙ ڍ ڊ ڏ ڌ ڇ ڃ ڦ ڻ ڱ ڳ ڪ) for sounds particular to Sindhi and other Indo-Aryan languages

              414

              The local ascents of Sindhi are ________?

              Correct Answer: All of the above
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              It has a total of 52 letters, augmenting the Persian with digraphs and eighteen new letters (ڄ ٺ ٽ ٿ ڀ ٻ ڙ ڍ ڊ ڏ ڌ ڇ ڃ ڦ ڻ ڱ ڳ ڪ) for sounds particular to Sindhi and other Indo-Aryan languages

              415

              Which is the second most spoken language in Pakistan __________?

              Correct Answer: Pashto
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Most spoken language: Punjabi

              Second most spoken: Pashto

              Third most spoken: Sindhi

              416

              Which language played key role in gaining freedom from British Raj?

              Correct Answer: Urdu
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The British Raj was the rule by the British Crown on the Indian subcontinent from 1858 to 1947.

              417

              Karakoram pass is between

              Correct Answer: None
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Karakoram Pass is a 5,540 m or 18,176 ft mountain pass between India and China in the Karakoram Range.  'Karakoram' literally means 'Black Gravel' in Mongolic. 

              Elevation: 5,540 m

              Location: China–India border

              Range: Karakoram

              418

              Which pass connects Mardan with Malakand:

              Correct Answer: Dargai Pass
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Dargai Pass connects mardan with malakand.

              419

              Which valley is located at the coast/end of Kurram Pass?

              Correct Answer: Kohat Valley
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Kohat Pass located: Elevation of 855m (2,805ft) above sea level (Khigana Mountains)

              420

              Burzil Pass is located in?

              Correct Answer: Himalaya
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Burzil Pass is a high mountain pass at an elevation of 4100m above sea level.

              Located in the Himalaya Range, near Gilgit.

              The road to the pass is metaled.


              421

              In Hindu Kush mountain all passes connect Pakistan with

              Correct Answer: Afghanistan
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              In the Hindu Kush, passes connect Pakistan with Afghanistan and China. 

              All passes in the Hindu Kush mountains connect Pakistan with:

              • China
              • Afghanistan
              • Tajikistan
              • Iran
              • Afghanistan.

              Additional information


              The Hindu Kush is a mountain range that runs from northeast to southwest, 

              The Hindu Kush range starts near the Pamir Mountains,

              It is an 800-kilometre-long (500 mi) mountain range in Central and South Asia to the west of the Himalayas.

              The highest peak of the Hindu Kush range in Pakistan is Tirich Mir

              Elevation:  (7,708) meters (25,289) 

              422

              Which of the following pass connects Chitral with Wahkan?

              Correct Answer: Baroghil Pass
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Important Passes of Pakistan

              1. Khyber Pass 

              connects: Peshawar to Kabul

              connects: Pakistan to Afghanistan

              Height 1070 meters, 3510 feet 


              2. Bolan Pass 

              Connects: JCB and Sibi with Quetta 

              Height: 1793 meters, 5884 feet 


              3. Lowari Pass 

              Connects: Chitral with Dir 

              Height: 3118 meters, 10230 feet 


              4. Broghil Pass 

              Location: Pakistan & Afghanistan border 

              Height: 3798 meters, 12460 feet 


              5. Malakand Pass 

              Connects: Dir with Chitral 

              Height: 1362 meters, 4468 feet 


              6. Shandur pass

              Connects: Gupis/Gilgit with Chitral 

              Height: 3700 meters, 12139 feet 


              7. Khanjerab Pass (top most in Pakistan) 

              Other name: Friendship Gate

              Connects: China with Pakistan 

              Height: 4693 meters, 15397 feet 


              8. Dorah pass

              Connects: Badakhshan province of Afghanistan with Chitral 

              Height: 4300 meters, 14108 feet 


              9. Babusar Pass 

              Connects: Mansehra/Abbotabad with Gilgit


              10. Kohat Pass

              Connects: Kohat with Peshawar 


              11. Khojak Pass

              Connects: Qilla Abdullah with Chaman


              12. Tochi Pass

              Connects: Ghazni with Bannu

              Correct Answer: Waziristan with Bannu

              426

              Which of following is the main cause of rain fall in summer in Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: Monsoon winds
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Monsoons cause wet and dry seasons throughout much of the tropics. Monsoons are most often associated with the Indian Ocean. Monsoons always blow from cold to warm regions.

              427

              In which country summer monsoon does not cause rain ?

              Correct Answer: England
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The summer monsoon is associated with heavy rainfall. It usually happens between April and September.

              428

              On which date Pakistan recorded Highest Ever Temperature?

              Correct Answer: 28 May 2017
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Highest Temperature in Pakistan

              Happened: 28 May 2017

              Location: Turbat, Balochistan

              Gauge: 53.5°C


               Note: It equaled the previous record which was set in Mohenjo Daro on May 27, 2010.

               


              429

              The constituency of Gilgit Baltistan Assembly is called ________?

              Correct Answer: GBLA
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The constituency of Gilgit Baltistan Assembly is called GBA.

              A total of 33 seats are in the assembly.


              430

              Pakistan is said to have decided to elevate which region to a full fledged province status?

              Correct Answer: Gilgit Baltistan
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Pakistan has decided to elevate the status of Gilgit-Baltistan to that of a full-fledged province.

              India has conveyed to Pakistan that the entire Union territories of Jammu and Kashmir and Ladakh, including the areas of Gilgit and Baltistan, are Integral parts of the country. Prime Minister of Pakistan Imran Khan is to announce constitutional rights for the province soon.

              *********


              431

              The place of Pakistan which has an absolute minimum temperature of -16C is?

              Correct Answer: Skardu
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • The place in Pakistan which has an absolute minimum temperature of -16C is Skardu
              • ***
              • The second coldest place in Pakistan is Skardu, located in the Gilgit Baltistan region
              The coldest place in PakistanLocationWeather
              SkarduGilgit−Baltistan-20°C
              AstoreGilgit−Baltistan-16°C
              KalaamSwat-10°C
              ParchinarKohat-10°C
              432

              Fairy Meadow is located in which district of Gilgit Baltistan

              Correct Answer: Diamer
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Fairy Meadows, is a grassland near one of the base camp sites of the Nanga Parbat, located in Diamer District, Gilgit-Baltistan.

              Established: 1995

              433

              Who was the acting President of Gilgit Baltistan before joining Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: Shah Raees khan
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Gilgit is the capital city of Gilgit–Baltistan, Pakistan.

              The city is located in a broad valley near the confluence of the Gilgit River and Hunza River.

              434

              On November 1, 2021, Gilgit-Baltistan celebrates its _________ independence day.

              Correct Answer: 74th
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The people of GB celebrate their Independence Day on Nov 1 as the Gilgit Scouts had liberated their homeland from the Dogra Raj on this day in 1947.

              On November 1, 1947, the people of the mountainous region revolted against the rule of Maharaja Hari Singh, the last Dogra ruler of Jammu state, and liberated 72,000 square kilometres of land.

              The region now connects Pakistan with China, Afghanistan and India.


              435

              Member of Gilgit Baltistan Assembly is called________?

              Correct Answer: Both
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The constituency of Gilgit Baltistan Assembly is called GBA.

              A total of 33 seats are in the assembly.

              436

              Skardu is district of۔

              Correct Answer: Gilgit−Baltistan
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Skardu is a city located in Gilgit−Baltistan, Pakistan, and serves as the capital of Skardu District and the Baltistan Division.

              Skardu is situated at an elevation of nearly 2,500 metres in the Skardu Valley, at the confluence of the Indus and Shigar Rivers.

              437

              The Ishkoman Valley is in___________?

              Correct Answer: Gilgit Baltistan
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Ishkoman valley lies in the north of Ghizer District in Gilgit-Baltistan,  bordering Afghanistan and the Pamir Wakhan Corridor. 

              438

              which of the following is the biggest province geological circumstance of Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: Gilgit Baltistan
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • Gilgit Baltistan is the biggest province geological circumstance of Pakistan
              • **
              • Gilgit-Baltistan is home to all five of Pakistan's "eight-thousanders" and to more than fifty peaks above 7,000 meters (23,000 ft).
              • Gilgit-Baltistan has been under Pakistan administration since 1947


              439

              Gilgit-Baltistan is celebrated its ________ independence day on 1 November 2022.

              Correct Answer: 75th
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Gilgit-Baltistan is celebrated its 75th independence day on 1 November 2022.

              On Nov 1, 1947, the Gilgit Scouts overthrew the government of Kashmir’s Dogra ruler.

               

              440

              How many districts are there in Gilgit Baltistan (GB)?

              Correct Answer: 14
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              All districts of Gilgit Baltistan:

              1.       Astore

              2.       Darel

              3.       Diamer

              4.       Ghanche

              5.       Ghizer

              6.       Gilgit

              7.       Gupis–Yasin

              8.       Hunza

              9.       Kharmang  

              10.   Nagar

              11.   Roundu

              12.   Shigar

              13.   Skardu

              14.   Tangir

              **

              Gilgit Baltistan is located on the North side of Pakistan

              Gilgit is a city of: Gilgit-Baltistan Pakistan

              Gilgit-Baltistan, formerly known as the Northern Areas, is a region administered by Pakistan as an administrative territory.

              Gilgit-Baltistan was Established on 1 November 1948

              Capital of Gilgit-Baltistan is Gilgit

                Largest city of Gilgit-Baltistan is Skardu

                ND-8-08-2023

                441

                The main source of revenue for federal government is :

                Correct Answer: Sales tax
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The three main sources of federal tax revenue are individual income taxes, payroll taxes, and corporate income taxes.

                Other sources of tax revenue include excise taxes, the estate tax, and other taxes and fees.

                442

                In Pakistan's federal system residuary powers are exercised by?

                Correct Answer: Centre
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                443

                Name of Army operation in FATA?

                Correct Answer: Zarb-e-Azb
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • On June 15, the ongoing Pakistan Army operation "Operation Zarb-e-Azb" (Sharp Strike) completed its first year.
                • The military operation is the first of its kind against the Islamist insurgents based in the North Waziristan district of the Federally Administered Tribal Areas (FATA) of Pakistan.
                • However, there have been previous operations elsewhere in FATA since Pakistan's first operation against al-Qaeda, Tarik-e-Taliban Pakistan (TTP) and other foreign Islamist militant groups in the area in 2002.


                444

                Total agencies of FATA are_______?

                Correct Answer: Seven
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • The area of ex-Federally Administrated Tribal Areas (FATA) is 27220 square kilometers.
                • ***
                • The Federally Administered Tribal Areas (FATA) consisted of two types of areas:
                • 1.      Tribal Agencies (Tribal Districts)
                • 2.      Frontier Regions (FRs).
                • There were seven Tribal Agencies and six Frontier Regions
                  • ****

                Related Question to FATA:

                • Share of FATA in NFC award 3%
                • Operation Zarb-e-Azb underwent in the former FATA. Azb was the name of the Sword of the Holy Prophet (PBUH)
                • Name of Army operation in FATA? Zarb-e-Azb
                • The major languages of FATA are Pashto
                • kPK seats before FATA merger _124
                • General seats in the KPK assembly after the merger of FATA are 145
                • FATA was merged with KPK under which constitutional amendment 25th
                • Ex-FATA comprises 7 Agencies









                445

                The total number of Agencies in Federally Administrated Tribal Areas is?

                Correct Answer: 7
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • The area of ex-Federally Administrated Tribal Areas (FATA) is 27220 square kilometers.
                • ***
                • The Federally Administered Tribal Areas (FATA) consisted of two types of areas:
                • 1.      Tribal Agencies (Tribal Districts)
                • 2.      Frontier Regions (FRs).
                • There were seven Tribal Agencies and six Frontier Regions
                  • ****

                Related Question to FATA:

                • Share of FATA in NFC award 3%
                • Operation Zarb-e-Azb underwent in the former FATA. Azb was the name of the Sword of the Holy Prophet (PBUH)
                • Name of Army operation in FATA? Zarb-e-Azb
                • The major languages of FATA are Pashto
                • kPK seats before FATA merger _124
                • General seats in the KPK assembly after the merger of FATA are 145
                • FATA was merged with KPK under which constitutional amendment 25th
                • Ex-FATA comprises 7 Agencies









                446

                What is the name of largest city of Azad Kashmir?

                Correct Answer: Muzaffarabad
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Muzaffarabad is the capital and largest city of Azad Kashmir, and the 60th largest in Pakistan.

                Azad Jammu and Kashmir abbreviated as AJK

                located:  the western portion of the larger Kashmir region

                AJK has been the subject of a dispute between India and Pakistan since 1947

                Established October 24, 1947 (Azad Kashmir Day)

                The Capital of AJK is Muzaffarabad

                Largest city of AJK is Muzaffarabad

                Government: Type Self-governing state under Pakistani administration

                Total Districts of AJK: 10

                Total Divisions of AJK: 3

                Total Tehsils of AJK: 33

                Team: AJK Jaguars

                447

                Sardar Abdul Qayyum Niazi become the ___ Prime minister of Azad Jammu and Kashmir (AJK)?

                Correct Answer: 13th
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Sardar Abdul Qayyum Niazi,  13th Prime Minister of Azad Kashmir (AJK) resigned on April 14, 2022


                Muzaffarabad is the capital of Azad Jammu and Kashmir.

                Area: 13297 Km

                Number of divisions: 3

                New Mir Pur City is the largest city of Azad Jammu and Kashmir.

                448

                India revoked special status of Kashmir?

                Correct Answer: 2019
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                On 5 August 2019, the Parliament of India voted in favour of a resolution tabled by Home Minister Amit Shah to revoke the temporary special status, or autonomy, granted under Article 370 of the Indian Constitution to Jammu and Kashmir

                _________________________________________________

                Districts of AJK

                Sr. No. District
                1 Muzaffarabad
                2 Hattian Bala
                3 Neelam Valley
                4 Mirpur
                5 Bhimber
                6 Kotli
                7 Poonch
                8 Bagh
                9 Haveli
                10 Sudhnati
                449

                Which are the Regional Languages of Kashmir?

                Correct Answer: Kashmiri
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                In 2020, the Parliament of India passed a bill to make Kashmiri an official language of Jammu and Kashmir along with Dogri, Hindi, English and Urdu.

                VU CBT FIA PAPER 16-12-2021
                450

                Azad Jammu and Kashmir observed ____________ liberation day on 24 October 2022.

                Correct Answer: 75th
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The 75th anniversary of the foundation of a free government in the erstwhile princely state of Jammu and Kashmir would be observed on October 24, 2022 with traditional fervour.

                On October 24, 1947, the free government was installed in this territory, commonly known as Azad Jammu and Kashmir (AJK), after its liberation by the locals from the yoke of Dogra rule, and Sardar Mohammad Ibrahim Khan (late) was appointed its founding president.

                451

                The largest District of AJK is------?

                Correct Answer: Neelum Valley
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Neelam Valley is the largest district of Azad Kashmir by area.

                The valley extends for approximately 200 kilometers along the Neelum River. 

                ****

                there are 10 districts of Azad Kashmir

                Kotli is the biggest district of Azad Kashmir by population

                Sudhnati is the smallest district by area

                Haveli is the smallest district in the population of Azad Kashmir.

                ***

                 


                •  ND03-12-2022
                452

                The ziarat Residence was once which Leader House?

                Correct Answer: Quaid e Azam
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Ziarat District was established in July 1986, previously being part of Sibi District.

                The district, with a population of 52855, is subdivided into two tehsils Ziarat and Sanjawi.

                The district contains a total of ten Union Councils.

                453

                Ziarat is a hill station in _______ division

                Correct Answer: Sibi
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Ziarat is a hill station in the Sibi division of the Balochistan province in Pakistan

                It was established in July 1986, previously being part of Sibi District.

                It is known as the “Fruit Basket of Pakistan” 

                It has subdivided into two tehsils Ziarat and Sanjawi.

                The district contains a total of ten Union Councils.


                454

                Which province of Pakistan is called "Land of Apples "?

                Correct Answer: Baluchistan
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                 Baluchistan is called land of Apples.

                455

                Veteran Baloch leader Dr Abdul Hayee died in a road accident on:

                Correct Answer: February 25, 2022
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Prominent Baloch politician and National Democratic Party (NDP) president Dr Abdul Hayee Baloch died in a road accident near Bahawalpur on 25-02-2022. He was 77.

                456

                Hingol Mud Volcanoes are in which Province Of Pakistan ?

                Correct Answer: Baluchistan
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The Hingol mud volcanoes are located in Lasbela District, Balochistan

                457

                Where the city Kharan is located?

                Correct Answer: Balochistan
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Kharan is in Rakhshan division of Balochistan.

                Kharan desert is also located in Balochistan.
                458

                Which city declared disaster struck after heavy rains in July 2022?

                Correct Answer: Quetta
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The Balochistan government declared Quetta a disaster struck area and imposed a state of emergency in the provincial capital after heavy rains killed 13 people in the province.

                Updated: 6 July 2022 (11.40 AM)

                459

                The construction of Khojak Tunnel completed in the year:

                Correct Answer: 1891
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The construction of Khojak Tunne started in 1888

                while 

                The construction of Khojak Tunne was completed in 1891

                Khojak Tunnel was once the 4th longest tunnel in the world



                Khojak Tunne

                The location of Khojak Tunne is

                Balochistan

                The length of Khojak Tunne is

                3.9 KM

                Khojak pass connects 

                Qila Abdullah with Chaman

                Khojak Tunne is the longest Railway Tunnel of

                Pakistan

                Khojak Tunne Also known as

                Shela Bagh Tunnel

                Khojak Tunnel is located near the city of

                West of Quetta

                Note: The State Bank of Pakistan recognized the marvel of the Khojak Tunnel and a picture of it was printed on the five-rupee note in 1976 which remained in circulation until 2005.


                ND-6-09-2023

                Khojak Tunnel is located near the city of:

                Correct Answer: Quetta
                Show explanation

                while 

                The construction of Khojak Tunne was completed in 1891

                 still is the longest tunnel built in Pakistan.

                465

                __________ out of 34 Balochistan districts declared `calamity-hit` in August 2022?

                Correct Answer: 32
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The Balochistan government declared 32 out of the total 34 provincial districts `calamity-hit` on August 31, 2022.

                466

                Mohammad Noor Meskanzai assassinated in October 2022, he had served chief justice of which province?

                Correct Answer: Balochistan
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Former Federal Shariat Court and Balochistan High Court chief justice Mohammad Noor Meskanzai was assassinated while offering prayer at a mosque in Kharan on 14 October 2022.

                Noor Meskanzai remained chief justice High Court from 2014 to 2018.

                What is the percentage of Balochistan area in Pakistan?

                Correct Answer: 42%
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • 42% of Balochistan area in Pakistan
                • **
                • Balochistan is located on the southeastern part of the Iranian plateau.
                • It borders the geopolitical regions of the Middle East and Southwest Asia, Central Asia, and South Asia.

                    35✔

                    And 7 Divisions

                    Provincial Capital: Quetta

                    468

                    Loralai is a district of which province?

                    Correct Answer: Balochistan
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Loralai District is a district in northeast of Balochistan province of Pakistan.

                    Loralai town is the district headquarters.


                    469

                    Which of the following became 35th district of Balochistan on 3 September 2022?

                    Correct Answer: Hub
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • The Balochistan government on September 3, 2022 notified Hub district, taking the total number of districts in the province to 35.
                    • The new district comprises Hub, Dureji and Sonmiani (Winder) subdivisions, and the modified Lasbela now comprises Bela, Uthal and Kanraj subdivisions with headquarters at Uthal
                    470

                    How many districts Makran division has?

                    Correct Answer: Three
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Makran division (Balochistan) has the following 3 districts:

                    1.       Turbat

                    2.       Punjgur

                    3.       Gwadar.

                    471

                    Govt. of Balochistan has created a new district _______ in Nasirabad division in May 2013?

                    Correct Answer: Lehri
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Lehri District was the 31st district in Balochistan, Pakistan.

                    It was created in May 2013 from parts of the districts of Sibi and Kachhi.

                    472

                    Muslim Bagh is famous for chromite and is located in?

                    Correct Answer: Balochistan
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Muslim Bagh is a town in Killa Saifullah District, Balochistan

                    It is famous for Chromite

                    It is located at an altitude of 1,787 meters (5,863 ft). 
                    Muslim Bagh was formerly called Hindu Bagh

                    473

                    Kalat and around area language is?

                    Correct Answer: Barahvi
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Kalāt or Qalāt historically known as Qīqān

                    Qalat  is a district in Balochistan

                    Kalat districts of Balochistan Province are predominantly Brahui-speaking

                    Kalat join Pakistan on 27 March 1948

                    Kalat is known locally as Kalat-e-Brahui and Kalat-e-Sewa

                     Kalat is the capital of the Kalat District 

                    Kalat is known for Juniper old trees, the Moro Rive

                    ND21-2-2023



                    474

                    Name the island of Pasni coast?

                    Correct Answer: Astola
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Astola Island is in Arabian Sea

                    Island means جزیرہ

                    According to the Survey of Pakistan, 

                    The new length of the coastline of Pakistan is 1058 km

                    the Old length of the coastline is 1046KM

                    **************

                    the coastline of Pakistan is bifurcated into two parts,

                    Sindh Coast  and Makran Coast (Baluchistan)258 km falling in the Sindh province and 800 km in Balochistan

                    The maximum part of this coastline is located in Balochistan.

                    Geographically, the coast of Pakistan

                    • Gwadar Coast
                    •  Lasbela Coast
                    •  Karachi Coast
                    • Thatta Coast from Korangi Creek up to the Indian border at Sir Creek
                    • Rann of Kutch from Badin to Tharparkar District


                    475

                    Bolan pass connect the Jacobabad and SIBI with?

                    Correct Answer: Quetta
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Karakar pass is located in: Hindu Kush, KPK

                     Important Passes of Pakistan

                    1. Khyber Pass 

                    connects: Peshawar to Kabul

                    connects: Pakistan to Afghanistan

                    Height 1070 meters, 3510 feet 


                    2. Bolan Pass 

                    Connects: JCB and Sibi with Quetta 

                    Height: 1793 meters, 5884 feet 


                    3. Lowari Pass 

                    Connects: Chitral with Dir 

                    Height: 3118 meters, 10230 feet 


                    4. Broghil Pass 

                    Location: Pakistan & Afghanistan border 

                    Height: 3798 meters, 12460 feet 


                    5. Malakand Pass 

                    Connects: Dir with Chitral 

                    Height: 1362 meters, 4468 feet 


                    6. Shandur pass

                    Connects: Gupis/Gilgit with Chitral 

                    Height: 3700 meters, 12139 feet 


                    7. Khanjerab Pass (top most in Pakistan) 

                    Other name: Friendship Gate

                    Connects: China with Pakistan 

                    Height: 4693 meters, 15397 feet 


                    8. Dorah pass

                    Connects: Badakhshan province of Afghanistan with Chitral 

                    Height: 4300 meters, 14108 feet 


                    9. Babusar Pass 

                    Connects: Mansehra/Abbotabad with Gilgit


                    10. Kohat Pass

                    Connects: Kohat with Peshawar 


                    11. Khojak Pass

                    Connects: Qilla Abdullah with Chaman


                    12. Tochi Pass

                    Connects: Ghazni with Bannu


                    13. Karakar Pass

                    Connects: Mingora with Buner 


                    **********

                    476

                    On which of the following languages alphabets the Sindhi Language is based?

                    Correct Answer: Sansikrat
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Sindhi language is now generally written in the Arabic script, but it belongs to the Indo-Aryan language family and over seventy percent of Sindhi words are of Sanskrit origin.

                    477

                    Approximately what percentage of children in Sindh are stunted?

                    Correct Answer: 46%
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Nearly five of every 10 children of five-year age or less in Sindh are stunted, while two of them suffer from wasting illnesses.

                    478

                    At present who holds the position of the leader of the house in Sindh Assembly:

                    Correct Answer: Murad Ali Shah
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Leader of the Opposition of Sindh Assembly

                    Haleem Adil Sheikh PTI since 26 January 2021




                    479

                    Kashmore is in:

                    Correct Answer: Sindh
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Kashmore is located near Jacobabad, the hottest city of Pakistan.

                    480

                    Who lead the foundation stone of Pakistan Steel Mills Corporation :

                    Correct Answer: Zulifiqar Ali Bhutto
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Pakistan Steel Mill was founded by Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto

                    It was founded on 30th December, 1973 

                    It is located in Karachi.

                    It is the country's largest industrial undertaking

                    Production Capacity: 1.1 Million Ton of Steel Expandable up to 3.0 Million Ton per annum.


                    481

                    Which of following industries has the largest production in Sindh?

                    Correct Answer: Cotton Fabric
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Sindh is Pakistan's most natural gas producing province. Agriculture is very important in Sindh with cotton, rice, wheat, sugar cane, bananas, and mangoes as the most important crops. Sindh is the richest province in natural resources of gas, petrol, and coal.

                    482

                    “Shaheed Benazirabad” is the new name of:

                    Correct Answer: Nawabshah
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The district Nawabshah was renamed as Shaheed Benazirabad in the year 2008.

                    483

                    Heer Ismail Soho set to become first-ever woman tribal head in Sindh. She belongs to the district:

                    Correct Answer: Thatta
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Pakistan Peoples Party (PPP) member of Sindh Assembly Heer Ismail Soho is going to become the first-ever woman chieftain of any tribe or clan in Sindh.

                    She got a Sindh Assembly seat reserved for women in 2018.

                    484

                    In March 2022, which Hindu teenage girl allegedly killed in Sukkur for refusing marry?

                    Correct Answer: Pooja Kumari
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    An 18-year-old girl from the Hindu community was killed on 21-03-2022 in Sukkur.

                    The assailants allegedly broke into Pooja Kumari's house and opened fire on her for refusing marry.

                    485

                    Which creek is located in Thatta district:

                    Correct Answer: Gharo creek
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Gharo has large bazar for shopping of local communities, sits on the delta of the Indus River, its economy is based on fishing and agriculture

                    486

                    The government of Sindh has recently introduced a new district in Karachi is___________?

                    Correct Answer: Kemari
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Sindh cabinet on Aug 20, 2020 approved to carve a new district in Keamari area by bifurcating Karachi’s District West.

                    Keamari is the 30th district of Sindh.

                    -------

                    Total District in Pakistan 136

                    Facts about Pakistan Azla/Districts

                    1. How many districts are in Punjab?

                    36✔

                    And 9 Divisions

                     

                    2. How many districts in KPK?

                    35 (after 25th amendment 1973- FATA merge)

                    And 7 Divisions

                     

                    3. How many districts in Baluchistan?

                    35✔

                    And 7 Divisions

                     

                    4. How many districts in Sindh?

                    30✔

                    And 7 Divisions


                    5. How many districts in Gilgit Baldistan?

                    10✔

                     

                    6. How many districts are in Azad Kashmir?

                    10✔

                     

                    7. The largest district of Pakistan by population?

                    Karachi✔(14.91 million)

                     

                    8. The smallest districts of Pakistan by area?

                    Tor Ghar✔

                    9. The largest districts of Pakistan by area?

                    Chagai✔

                      

                    10. The largest districts of Punjab by area?

                    Bhalwarpur✔

                    487

                    Which of the following distinguishes the Harappan from other contemporary civilizations of the world?

                    Correct Answer: Town planning and drainage
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Harappan civilization was located in the Indus River valley. Its two large cities, Harappa and Mohenjo-daro, were located in present-day Pakistan's Punjab and Sindh provinces, respectively.

                    488

                    Which of the following fort is known as Fort Ahmadabad?

                    Correct Answer: Kot Diji
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Kot Diji Fort Location: Khairpur District, Sindh

                    489

                    Sassui Pannuh is Sindhi Story, Sassui is Known as_________?

                    Correct Answer: Princess of Hope
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Sassi Punnuh is a love story from Sindhi and Balochi folklore.

                    The story also appears in Shah Jo Risalo and forms part of seven popular tragic romances from Sindh, Pakistan.

                    490

                    Sehwan city is famous for its mausoleum of ______________.

                    Correct Answer: Sh. Usman Merwandi
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Hazrat Sayyid Usman Marwandi, popularly known as Lal Shahbaz Qalandar , was a Sufi saint and poet of present-day Pakistan and Afghanistan.

                    Born Usman 1177 Maiwand

                    Died 19 February 1274 Sehwan 

                    Religion Islam

                    Sehwan  is a historic city located in Jamshoro District of Sindh.


                    491

                    The longest Railway platform of Pakistan ?

                    Correct Answer: Rohri
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The platform of Rohri Railway Station  with length of about 1,894 feet. 

                    492

                    After the abolition of One Unit Scheme, in which year Sindh regained the Provincial Status in the year:

                    Correct Answer: 1970
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The One Unit program remained in effect until 1970.

                    Sindh regained the Provincial Status in 1970.


                    493

                    Hindu Marriage Bill 2016 was passed by:

                    Correct Answer: Singh Assembly
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Pakistan has Bicameral legislation

                    494

                    Which among the following claimed responsibility for Karachi University suicide blast in April 2022?

                    Correct Answer: BLA
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    1 Pakistani and three Chinese have been killed.

                    Baloch Liberation Army, an outfit organization claimed responsibility for this attack.

                    Blast Took place: 26-04-2022


                    495

                    Gibraltar of Sindh is called to _________?

                    Correct Answer: Makli Hills
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Makli Necropolis is one of the largest funerary sites in the world, spread over an area of 10 kilometres near the city of Thatta, in the Pakistani province of Sindh.

                    496

                    Where ‘White Desert’ is situated in the province of Sindh?

                    Correct Answer: Khair Pur Mirs
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    This beauty desert is called ‘Achro Thar’ in Sindhi language.

                    Khairpur Mirs is a district of Sindh.

                    497

                    In whose era, Jehandad Khan was appointed Governor of Sindh?

                    Correct Answer: Zia-Ul-Haq
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Lieutenant-General (retired) Jahan Dad Khan remained Governor Sindh from April 1984 to January 1987.

                    498

                    The second phase of Local Government elections scheduled to take place in Sindh on _________ 2022.

                    Correct Answer: August 28
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The second phase of local government elections in Sindh would be held on August 28, 2022.

                    By-elections on a vacant seat of NA 245, Karachi would be held on August 21, 2022.

                     

                    499

                    Urs of great Saint Hazrat Syed Abdullah Shah Ghazi began on 20 July 2022 in the city:

                    Correct Answer: Karachi
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                     Three-day annual Urs of great Saint Hazrat Syed Abdullah Shah Ghazi is being held in Clifton area of Karachi from 20 July 22 July 2022

                    500

                    Kalan kot fort is situated near?

                    Correct Answer: Thatta
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Kalan Kot Fort was built probably in 14th century along with Thatta.

                    It is located 5km south of Makli.
